programme.desc: Dr Jeff performs a risky operation to save a puppys leg - but whether the pooch will walk again is another matter. Plus, an abandoned cats x-ray tells a shocking story

programme.title: Meet the Orangutans
programme.desc: The orphans are learning how to make important relationships. Mui Mui and Anne have become inseparable, but now Anne is pregnant, and she needs to be separated

programme.desc: The team is on a roll at Gold Run, and things are beginning to look up. But everyone is forced to evacuate the camp when freezing winter storms approach

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: The brothers-in-law team up with a company to build a one-of-a-kind table tank for a Las Vegas trade show. Later, they create a replica crabbing boat aquarium

programme.title: Shark Lockdown

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How can a fish fly the length of three football fields? Can a spider be adorable? Whats the real story behind a flamingos colour?

programme.title: Meet the Superbrains

programme.title: Tanked
programme.sub-title: For Love of the Game - or Money?
programme.desc: Brett Raymer and Wayde King create a bi-level koi fish pond for NFL star Bart Scott, and are challenged to build an acrylic tank guarded by sharks inside a vault

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Investigators respond to reports of an injured dog and are shocked to find that its paw is almost severed

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Liz Pavlicek visits a house where more than 30 spaniels have been neglected, and is shocked by their appalling conditions

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Tia is faced with her biggest rescue with a call about abandoned dogs - one of the worst cases of neglect she has ever seen

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: A woman turns to behaviourist Jackson Galaxy for help controlling her vicious feline, which is posing a threat to her 95-year-old mother

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy deals with a cat that behaves strangely around its stuffed animal, and another that viciously attacks a gentle, senior feline

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: In Calhoun County, Ben and Chelsea Bailey are out patrolling shrimp boats when one makes a run for it. Another warden makes some new furry friends

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Denver pet doc Jeff Young treats a puppy suffering from a deadly virus, and he must gain the trust of rescued wolves to help an ailing member of the pack

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson builds an adventure-themed treehouse for a family of explorers in Pennsylvanias Pocono mountains, packing the two-storey creation with novelties

programme.title: Shark Bait
programme.desc: Documentary in which Dr Greg Skomal and his team head to Cape Cod to examine the the territorial struggle between seals and great white sharks

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: The Kilchers need help from a helicopter to complete critical work, and Atz Lee makes progress with the cabin alone as tensions continue to mount with Jane

programme.title: Alaska: Surviving the Last Frontier
programme.sub-title: What Did We Get Ourselves Into?
programme.desc: Greg Pierson sets out on a mountain goat hunt despite never having fired a gun before, as the team races to gather food before the winter arrives

programme.title: Alaska Monsters
programme.desc: The outdoorsmen head into dangerous bear country to investigate a creature known as Thunderfoot that has been attacking grizzly bears

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: Soil poisoned by coal production hampers a Kentucky homestead. The Raneys must build a hillside garden on the only unpolluted land and find a source of clean water

programme.title: 100 Days Wild
programme.desc: Under pressure to catch a moose, Andrew and Gerrid must also contend with Olivers stubbornness. Later, Adam works on different building projects for base camp

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson explores the treetop getaways of Brazil, meeting South American treehouse builder Ricardo Brunelli, and travelling from the city of Sao Paulo to the farmlands of Parana

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: The investigators of the Houston SPCA look back on some of the most shocking cases of animal cruelty, from the thousands dealt with each year

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Chief investigator Jim Boller leads the mission to rescue a deer that has sustained severe injuries after running through a plate-glass window at an office

programme.title: Dance Passion Swansea
programme.desc: World-class dancers perform throughout the city of Swansea, such as the Ballet Cymru, who give Dance of the Knights from Prokofievs Romeo and Juliet a uniquely Welsh take. Freelance dancer and choreographer Joe Powell-Main performs a solo piece, incorporating Latin and ballroom with a twist. This programme also features a recital of Dylan Thomas Fern Hill, narrated by Cerys Matthews

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: When her nieces son is charged with possession of an ecstasy tablet, suspicions remain that he has been framed, so Hetty goes undercover in his school as a cafeteria worker to clear his name. Patricia Routledge stars

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: A 1976 edition filmed at the Varieties Theatre Leeds, in which chairman Leonard Sachs introduces old-time cabaret performances featuring Edward Woodward, Larry Grayson, Frank Carson, Margaret Savage, Sheila Mathews, Ted Durante and Hilda, Peter Gale and Albert Aldred

programme.title: Planet Earth III
programme.sub-title: Planet Earth III: Wonders of Nature
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents a compilation of highlights from the natural history documentary. In the Namib Desert, a pair of ostriches raise their family in the searing heat to keep them safe from predators - but time is ticking as the chicks begin to hatch. In the cold waters of North America, a little horn shark stumbles into an ambush set by an angel shark - but the potential victim has a secret weapon. In Botswana, newly hatched lily-trotter chicks learn how to walk on water, and in Bali, long-tailed macaques steal tourists valuable items - which they use to barter with staff for food

programme.title: David Attenborough: A Life on Air
programme.desc: In 1952, David Attenborough was turned down for a job with BBC radio, only to end up working for the corporations fledgling TV service - eventually becoming director of programmes. Since then he has become one of the most respected wildlife film-makers in the business, fronting classic series such as Life on Earth, The Blue Planet and Planet Earth. He talks to Michael Palin about his time at the corporation. First broadcast in 2002

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: The first episode of the landmark natural history series, with David Attenborough exploring the wildlife and landscape that inspired Charles Darwins theory of evolution. First broadcast in 1979

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following two tigers released into the wild to breed, where they must fight leopards for territory, learn to hunt boar and try to avoid being killed by poachers

programme.title: The Magic of Dance with Margot Fonteyn
programme.desc: Margot travels to Tchaikovskys house near Moscow, meeting Marie Rambert to discuss the life and legacy of Sergei Diaghilev, the founder of the Ballets Russes. The programme includes footage of Anna Pavlova, as well as Mikhal Baryshnikov dancing Petrushka

programme.title: Omnibus
programme.desc: A 1995 edition of the show looking at how the pairs musicals were made, featuring anecdotes from Rogers and clips from Astaires last TV interview

programme.title: Puttin on the Ritz: The Genius of Fred Astaire
programme.desc: A short compilation of clips from actor and dancer Fred Astaires days at RKO studios, where he formed an unforgettable partnership with Ginger Rogers

programme.title: An Art Lovers Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke embark on more cultural city breaks, beginning by heading to the Portuguese capital to see what it has to offer. From a spectacular monument to the maritime globetrotting of Portugals golden age and the work of a photographer documenting the citys large African population, they discover a complex history of former glories and a darker, slave-trading past

programme.title: Planet Earth III
programme.sub-title: Planet Earth III: Wonders of Nature
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents a compilation of highlights from the natural history documentary. In the Namib Desert, a pair of ostriches raise their family in the searing heat to keep them safe from predators - but time is ticking as the chicks begin to hatch. In the cold waters of North America, a little horn shark stumbles into an ambush set by an angel shark - but the potential victim has a secret weapon. In Botswana, newly hatched lily-trotter chicks learn how to walk on water, and in Bali, long-tailed macaques steal tourists valuable items - which they use to barter with staff for food

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: The first episode of the landmark natural history series, with David Attenborough exploring the wildlife and landscape that inspired Charles Darwins theory of evolution. First broadcast in 1979

programme.title: David Attenborough: A Life on Air
programme.desc: In 1952, David Attenborough was turned down for a job with BBC radio, only to end up working for the corporations fledgling TV service - eventually becoming director of programmes. Since then he has become one of the most respected wildlife film-makers in the business, fronting classic series such as Life on Earth, The Blue Planet and Planet Earth. He talks to Michael Palin about his time at the corporation. First broadcast in 2002

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the fifth of the academic quizs first-round matches, four students from Cranfield University pit their wits against a team from Royal Holloway, University of London, in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the quiz show of patience and lateral thinking, with the County Councillors taking on the Ombudsmen, trying to make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Ancients
programme.desc: Profile of the ancient Hittite civilisation, looking at what archaeologists found when they uncovered its lost capital, Hattusha in modern-day Turkey, including temples, palaces and a pyramid-like structure facing Egypt. The historians also discovered a library charting the rise and fall of the empire, which vanished 3,000 years ago. Narrated by Mark Halliley

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the history of the North European region through its art. He begins by examining the links between landscape, climate and national character, taking in everything from the era of the Vikings to Edvard Munchs The Scream

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.sub-title: Jodrell Bank: Tuning into the Universe
programme.desc: The team explores the story of Jodrell Bank Observatory and its towering Lovell telescope, which scientists have used to listen to the cosmos for almost 70 years. Maggie Aderin meets research scientist Emmanuel K Bempong-Manful to discover who decides who gets time on the renowned telescope, and what it can reveal. Chris Lintott joins Danielle George, GCHQs chief scientific adviser for national security, to examine previously top-secret files revealing Jodrell Banks role at the height of Cold War tensions

programme.title: British Art at War: Bomberg, Sickert and Nash
programme.sub-title: David Bomberg - Prophet In No Mans Land
programme.desc: Presenter Andrew Graham-Dixons assessment of three artists responses to the First World War concludes as he focuses on the work of David Bomberg. Recognised as one of the most original painters of his generation, Bombergs early modernist works pushed art to its limits. Born in Londons East End, the artist spent his life searching for order in an increasingly disordered world, and his journey took him as far as Palestine before settling in Spain, where he died in 1957

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: Examining the decisive shift of power from countryside to the city during the 19th century. With the industrial revolution transforming the British Isles, a divide opened up between city and country, forcing artists to respond to the upheaval to lives and the landscape. Contributors include Icelandic artist Olafur Eliasson, who reflects on the inspiration of JMW Turner, arguably the first environmental artist

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.sub-title: Jodrell Bank: Tuning into the Universe
programme.desc: The team explores the story of Jodrell Bank Observatory and its towering Lovell telescope, which scientists have used to listen to the cosmos for almost 70 years. Maggie Aderin meets research scientist Emmanuel K Bempong-Manful to discover who decides who gets time on the renowned telescope, and what it can reveal. Chris Lintott joins Danielle George, GCHQs chief scientific adviser for national security, to examine previously top-secret files revealing Jodrell Banks role at the height of Cold War tensions

programme.title: Dam Busters Declassified
programme.desc: Actor Martin Shaw takes to the skies in a bid to separate the facts from the myths surrounding the famous 1943 raid by 617 Squadron, which used bouncing bombs to attack German dams. He also talks to the last living RAFT veteran of the mission, as well as to a survivor of the flooding caused by the breaching of the Moehne dam, and meets the secret wartime girlfriend of pilot Guy Gibson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Brentford at Etihad Stadium, and Liverpool v Chelsea at Anfield. The Citizens had already registered two victories over the Bees in the league and Carabao Cup this term, and were hoping for a third as they continue their quest for a ninth Premier League title, and a seventh in nine years. Meanwhile, the Reds were looking to take advantage of another period of instability for the Blues who have seen two head coach departures in this campaign. Plus, action from Brighton and Hove Albion v Wolverhampton Wanderers, Fulham v Bournemouth, and Sunderland v Manchester United

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: Interviews with key politicians and public figures

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week, with policy-makers questioned on the key issues

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Tommy Walsh catches up on the progress made on a multimillion-pound project in London that he first saw in 2021 as it reaches its conclusion and Martel Maxwell encounters two characterful projects in North Yorkshire and Cumbria

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapmans buyers are renting in the countryside of Herefordshire, but they need her help to find them a house of their own that comes with enough land for a holiday let. Watch out for a surprise visit from another of the shows presenters, who also famously lives in the county

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher visits the historic city of Lincoln to explore its faith and worship music, including a thriving network of churches with a total congregation of over 2,000 people

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Despicable Me 3 (2017)
programme.desc: Dismissed from the Anti-Villain League after botching a mission, Gru sets off to meet his long-lost twin brother for one final criminal mission. Animated sequel, with the voices of Steve Carell, Kristen Wiig, Trey Parker and Miranda Cosgrove

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how a remote garden on Scotlands west coast has become an oasis for barn owls and pine martens as they battle the elements. During the brief summer, crossbills pry seeds from the cones in specially planted Scots pine trees, house martins raise their chicks, reptiles are finally warm enough to breed, and carnivorous sundews trap insects in a specially created bog garden

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Wild London
programme.desc: Having lived in London for 75 years, David Attenborough shares his intimate knowledge of the citys natural history. London is considered the greenest city in the world with a surprising variety of animal dramas playing out in the most unexpected places - whether its pigeons commuting by the Tube, snakes slithering along Regents canal, parakeets raiding city parks or beavers building a home next to a busy shopping centre. The veteran naturalist celebrates the extraordinary ways animals adapt to survive in the urban jungle and the inspiring initiatives to encourage nature back into the city

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Adam Henson visit Oswestry Livestock Market, one of the regions largest weekly cattle and sheep markets. As spring trading peaks, they step into a fast-moving world where fortunes can change in minutes. With livestock numbers falling and markets closing nationwide, Oswestry remains a crucial lifeline, not just for trade, but for farming communities to come together

programme.title: British Academy Television Awards
programme.desc: Greg Davies hosts the annual ceremony from the Southbank Centres Royal Festival Hall. Dramas hoping to win include Blue Lights, Code of Silence, This City Is Ours, A Thousand Blows, I Fought the Law, Trespasses, What it Feels Like for a Girl and Adolescence, while the acting categories see Aimee Lou Wood and Erin Doherty going up against each twice, in both the leading and supporting actress categories

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: Matty flails as Ning continues to push him. Meanwhile, Leanne and Gary bond, until he pressures her to betray Matty to safeguard her family

programme.title: Bikes!
programme.desc: Stephen Watson presents from the second day of the annual motorcycle event on a nine-mile course running between Portstewart, Coleraine and Portrush, featuring the Supertwin/Sportbike, Superbike and Supersport races

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Arsenal at London Stadium and Burnley v Aston Villa at Turf Moor. The relegation-threatened Hammers took on their title-chasing London rivals, and the Villans continued their bid to secure Champions League football against a Burnley side already resigned to playing in the Championship next term. Plus, Crystal Palace v Everton and Nottingham Forest v Newcastle United

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern hosts, with guest panellists comedian Jon Richardson and actress Sheila Hancock joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the weeks news

programme.desc: Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley present the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Toy Company Bullied Me Into Paying Unlawful Fees
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate how a firm providing equipment to children with special educational needs has let down dozens of families

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin tackles an overgrown ivy thats invading an end-of-terrace house in north Wales and visits a bungalow in Stoke with a damp problem. Martel Maxwell visits a former retail premises in Nottinghamshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper visits Railway Wood, a replica First World War trench in Kent based on one at the Battle of Passchendaele in Belgium. Meanwhile, two teams of antiques hunters go shopping for collectibles with the help of Kate Bliss and Mark Stacey

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: Rav and Nick battle to stop a remote-access broadband scam, while interceptor Mark investigates sextortion scammers who blackmail victims with threats to expose intimate images

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman presents the first in a run of episodes in which famous faces decide whether to relocate to the countryside. Former England international Neil Razor Ruddock find his softer side, as he and his wife Leah look for properties in rural Kent. Razors tough guy persona is put to the test when a 15th-century thatched cottage causes him concern about spiders, while Leah has aspirations to rear some animals in their new country pad

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps mother and daughter Diana and Millie seek out space to bloom in the Somerset countryside and also meets a couple who have planted their own vineyard

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.sub-title: Yorkshire - Crumbling York Minster Gets Rescued
programme.desc: York Minster is one of the UKs most magnificent cathedrals, boasting the worlds largest expanse of medieval stained glass. However, the stonework of this glorious gothic monument is crumbling away on a daily basis. Dominic Chinea and Will Kirk join the new generation of stonemasons being trained to save it, harnessing modern technology while still using centuries-old skills

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: An intrepid team of four basketball players competes for cash by crossing a bridge of stepping stones across the studio floor. Quiz show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon on the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian makes a worrying discovery, Nicola takes matters into her own hands, and Grant helps a friend

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Grace Dent and Anna Haugh present the last round of heats, with six more amateur chefs serving up a dinner party menu that will have guests begging for the recipe. The dishes in this round include a posh cheese sandwich and a middle eastern dessert. With the two best efforts granted a place in the quarter finals, the remaining four must prove their worth with a meringue roulade

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Shannon and Dylan finally spend time together. He wants to mend their relationship, but she is not ready to listen, until a journey into his past helps her understand. Meanwhile, a different side of Ollie comes to light as she encounters an old foe. Cats insecurities get the better of her, and she doesnt know where to turn, until she and Ollie finally find common ground

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Shannon is ready to take control of her life, even if it means building dangerous new alliances. Determined to protect her family, she turns her attention to Sam. As chaos builds, she, Cat and Ollie become increasingly desperate, until Shannon makes a bold decision

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: On patrol in Belfast, Perri and Adam respond to reports of cars performing dangerous stunts on the streets while large crowds look on, but the cars scatter when they arrive and the officers dont catch anyone. The team also receives reports of two women being disruptive at a hotel

programme.title: Bikes!
programme.desc: Stephen Watson presents from the third day of the annual motorcycle event on a nine-mile course running between Portstewart, Coleraine and Portrush, featuring the Superstock, Supertwin/Sportbike and Superbike races

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high stakes quiz. Actor Charlie Brooks, Dr Amir Khan, presenter Sara Damergi and comedian Tim Vine must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for the charity of their choice with each correct answer

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Charlie Coopers Myth Country
programme.desc: The writer and actor tours the UK in his campervan, exploring local legends, myths and folklore and gathering evidence in a bid to get to the truth. He begins by going in search of Black Shuck, the demon dog of East Anglia, attempting an overnight stakeout to capture it on film, with unexpected consequences

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Louise makes a distressing discovery about her pregnancy. Gaz trains Munch to be his best man and Janet picks an unconventional theme for Donnas wedding - Dead Jonny

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Gaz and Donnas wedding turns out to be a morbid affair, prompting the groom to draw up a list of things to do before he dies and his bride to confront her fear of death by taking a job in a charity shop. Janet sees Munch in one of Johnnys old tracksuits, and Louise makes a shocking discovery about Corinthian

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Just as Janet begins to feel ready to go on with her life after Jonnys death, Louise reveals she has been hiding all of her post for six months - and among the unopened letters is a repossession order for Janets house. With only 24 hours to save her home, she seeks the help of Aidan, a bumbling local villain who has come into money after robbing a post office

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: While Donna is away in London at a job interview, Gaz enlists Janets aid to inject some much-needed sophistication into his new home, but as the two decorate, their mutual attraction begins to resurface. Louise is alone when she goes into labour, and attempts to become her own birthing partner

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Penny decides to throw a party, hoping one of the male guests will turn out to be a suitable partner for Miranda. The singleton is mortified at the idea and invents a number of increasingly bizarre excuses to avoid attending the event. Starring Miranda Hart, Patricia Hodge, Sally Phillips and Adrian Scarborough

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: A handsome stranger loses his wallet in the joke shop, where it is found by Stevie and Miranda - who immediately begin trying to find out which one of them he would prefer to go on a date with. Meanwhile, Gary is offered a new job in Hong Kong, but seems to be waiting for someone to persuade him to stay. Starring Miranda Hart, Sarah Hadland and Tom Ellis

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Nicola is shocked when she receives news about a face from the past, Phil and Sam support a loved one, and Ian desperately tries to gain extra votes

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Nicola is forced to come clean to George, Ian makes a rash decision after being humiliated by Oscar, and Grant gives Linda food for thought with Johnny after Elaine tells her he has money problems

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Walford is rife with gossip as Beas anonymous video of Elaine gains traction online. Ian instantly regrets his actions, Grant steps in to help a friend, while Gina and Harry are forced to come clean

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It is election day, and Ian and Elaine anxiously await the results, while Max and Cindy celebrate their joint birthday party in Harrys Barn, but tensions grow between him and Linda

On April 6, 1917, Lance Corporal Tom Blake and Lance Corporal William Schofield receive orders from General Erinmore to prevent Colonel Mackenzie from leading The 2nd Devons into a trap set by the Germans. The soldiers must proceed on foot into enemy territory to deliver the message, which will stop 1,600 men from walking straight into a deadly trap. Sam Mendes war drama, starring Dean-Charles Chapman, George MacKay, Daniel Mays and Colin Firth

programme.title: James Arthur: Out of Our Minds
programme.desc: Middlesbrough-born singer James Arthur opens up about his mental health struggles and issues he has had with antidepressants as he prepares to go out on tour. He also investigates male mental health, speaking to others like him who have found it hard to stop taking antidepressants, but have instead found other ways to help themselves and learn to thrive

programme.title: Roman Kemp: The Fight for Young Lives
programme.desc: Broadcaster Roman Kemp knows first-hand the impact of serious mental health issues. In 2020, he made Our Silent Emergency in the wake of the death of a friend. He encouraged those in need to talk to friends, family and professionals, but now strives to move the conversation on from talking about mental health to discovering what action is needed to support those suffering

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tir Dhondy goes further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting locals

programme.title: Charlie Coopers Myth Country
programme.desc: The writer and actor heads to Wiltshire, home to ancient sites such as Stonehenge, Avebury and the Westbury chalk horse, to get to the bottom of why humans feel the need to leave marks on the landscape. He turns his investigation towards the far more recent phenomenon of crop circles, hoping to settle a debate that has plagued his family for years. They think that crop circles are the work of aliens, but Charlie strongly doubts that belief

programme.title: Charlie Coopers Myth Country
programme.desc: The actor travels to Wales, the land of dragons and song, on a mission to discover if King Arthur was a real man or just a myth and if theres any truth behind this treasure story. In the process, he meets treasure hunters of different sorts, a recorder of rare and ancient folk songs, and finds what he refers to as his tribe, a metal-detecting club from south Wales. Hes optimistic he will return home a rich man - if not in wealth, then certainly in knowledge

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Time Lord finds himself on board a rebel spaceship surrounded by a fleet of his greatest enemies, coming face to face with that rarest of things - a Dalek that has developed a conscience. The only way to get to the bottom of the mystery is for the Docxtor and Clara to be shrunk miniaturised and venture inside the Dalek to see what makes it tick . Sci-fi adventure, starring Peter Capaldi and Jenna Coleman, with Zawe Ashton and Michael Smiley

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Time Lord and Clara land in 12th-century Sherwood Forest, where they meet none other than Robin Hood - despite the Doctors insistence that the legendary hero never actually existed. Theres also a Sheriff of Nottingham, who has gained control of dangerous alien technology. Sci-fi adventure, starring Peter Capaldi and Jenna Coleman, with Tom Riley and Ben Miller

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to the Mexican island of Cabo Pulmo, where the people have worked tirelessly to bring their surrounding oceans back to life and make it a marine reserve

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall meets the wild dogs of southern Africa, finding out why their survival is so important from the people working to save them

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Louise is worried after Phils meeting with social services and fears she will be sent back into care. Meanwhile, Fatboy challenges Liam to a game of rounders, and several of the locals get involved, but Darren is more interested in convincing Jodie he wants a relationship with her

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The search for Lola takes Phil and Sharon to the house where her friend Alexa is staying, but the teenage mum remains one step ahead of them. As Phil opens up about his feelings, the pair are surprised by how close they have become. Back in Albert Square, Cora insists on staying at Dots house so she can catch the thief and clear her name, while Denise and Ian prepare to spend the night together - but not in the way they might have expected

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: On the day of the wedding, Sharon tries to mask her guilt, while Keanu uninvites Bernie for fear that his secret isnt safe. Cindy sneaks away from Christmas dinner to invite George over for a drink, leaving Elaine and Ian feeling jealous. Sukis plans to leave Walford for good hit a bump in the road and the Mitchells receive a surprise video message from Lola. At the Vic, life changes forever for Denise, Kathy, Linda, Sharon, Stacey and Suki

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The police have some shocking news for Sonia, and while Denise is torn between two lovers, both of them face off in The Vic. Elsewhere, theres a heartwarming moment for Martin and Stacey as they take a trip down memory lane

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: An incident at Walford High sparks a heated debate among the Albert Square locals

programme.title: EastEnders Investigates: The Manosphere
programme.desc: An investigation into the issues of misogyny and toxic masculinity, originally broadcast to tie in with the October 2025 EastEnders storyline centred on teenager Joel Marshall, played by Max Murray

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part one of two. Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. The first episode explores the risk of crime faced by tourists and visits a pool party where massive screens show scores of live sports events from around the world so that partygoers can bet from the comfort of their sun lounger

programme.title: P Diddy: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Yinka Bokinni investigates how Sean Combs has gone from being the worlds biggest hip-hop mogul to haemorrhaging money, businesses, friends and fans in a matter of months. Yinka will examine how Combs made it to the top, and what some may have turned a blind eye to along the way. Speaking to some of Combs inner circle, and biggest critics, Yinka will find out what they think about him and ask why so many are only speaking out now

programme.title: Charlotte in Sunderland
programme.desc: Charlotte Crosby juggles the demands running her business while being a mother to seven month-old Alba and introducing partner Jake to the joys of the Karma Sutra - all while cooking a fry-up

programme.title: Charlotte in Sunderland
programme.desc: Charlotte pays an emotional tribute to Nana Jean by having a tattoo with her ashes. Despite getting a harsh reality check about the company finances from dad Gary, Charlotte sets her sites on the most extravagant Ibiza trip ever

programme.title: Charlotte in Sunderland
programme.desc: Charlotte and a crew of 16 head to Ibiza for a no expenses spared photo shoot, her biggest ever. The pressure is on as she takes the reins but the distraction of the Ibizan nightlife threatens to throw the shoot off course

programme.title: Charlotte in Sunderland
programme.desc: Jake struggles to organise a surprise party for Charlotte. Then, as the skies open, the summer fashion launch fails to go according to plan. Is this the end of Charlottes business empire?

programme.title: Charlotte in Sunderland
programme.desc: Emotions are high as Alba Jean starts nursery and the numbers are in from the summer launch. Jake goes shopping for a new luxury vehicle for his business and, like most new parents, Charlotte and Jake are struggling to squeeze in date nights - so decide to organise some blind dates

programme.title: Charlotte in Sunderland
programme.desc: Charlotte takes Jake and Alba with her on a top-secret work trip to Australia. Letitia is awaiting the results of a mammogram and, to help keep her distracted, Gary books them in for a pamper day at the place where they got married

programme.title: Margrete: Queen of the North (2021)
programme.desc: In 1402, Queen Margrete has achieved what no one has managed before, gathering Denmark, Norway and Sweden into a peace-oriented union that she rules through her adopted son King Erik. But a conspiracy is in the making, and she finds herself in an impossible dilemma that could shatter her lifes work. Period drama, starring Trine Dyrholm and Morten Hee Andersen. In Danish

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Reeling from a sudden loss, Shannon lashes out at her family. Only Ollie can offer her comfort, and as her day takes a surreal turn, she reconnects with herself and confronts her fears, at least until Dylans return snaps her back into a painful reality

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Dylans new life brings moments of comfort, but he also finds it challenging in a way he never saw coming. His old coping mechanisms no longer work, and he is forced to ask himself what he truly wants. A conversation with Luke reminds him whats at stake and what actually matters. Meanwhile, Cat seeks clarity from her son, hoping to understand the future she wants for herself and her family

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Take a Hike
programme.desc: Last to lead a walk is 32-year-old Rob from Dundee, who works for a communications company and plays amputee football for Partick Thistle. Hes leading the walkers to one of the Highlands best-known spots, Glenfinnan Viaduct, as featured in the Harry Potter movies

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Vick Hope explore the chalk landscapes of the Chilterns, where rare grasslands, seasonal streams and specialised wildlife all depend on the fragile ground. Matt meets farmers working together to reconnect these landscapes, while Vick visits a rare winterbourne stream that flows only after winter rains. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes a rare breed Middle White pig to his Cotswolds farm

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond heads to Ben Ledi in the Trossachs to help out with some high-altitude fieldwork, while Arlene Stuart revisits Landwards coverage of the 2001 foot-and-mouth outbreak and finds out about a legacy of the crisis thats helping farmers now. Rosie Morton is in Gairloch, Wester Ross, to hunt for herring and find out how this over-exploited fish is making a comeback, while Leanna Fernandez is in Fife to join a team researching the movements of snipe. Cammy Wilson visits an Ayrshire couple determined to preserve and promote the Clydesdale horse

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Planting borders and planning for cut flowers are on Lizzie Schofield and Brian Cunninghams to-do list at Beechgrove Garden. Lizzie is direct sowing in the new cuttings garden, while Brian is in the zig-zag border planting varieties that will grow tall, and so are ideal for the back of a plot, but that dont require support or staking. Brian also kicks off this years collection of plants being cultivated in the 6x8 glasshouse, while Lizzie is the first of the presenters to start work in her competition plot. Plus, a report on the alpine collection at the Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents more highlights, with recipes from Big Zuu, Sophie Wyburd, Tony Singh and Samin Nosrat, while comedian Iain Stirling faces his food heaven or hell

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britains baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Tea with Mussolini (1999)
programme.desc: Eccentric English and American women living in Florence during the tense pre-war years of the 1930s experience the ups and downs of parenthood when they collectively adopt a local boy disowned by his father. However, as international hostilities break out, their happy existence comes under threat. Period comedy drama, starring Maggie Smith, Judi Dench, Joan Plowright, Cher, Lily Tomlin and Baird Wallace

programme.title: Badminton Horse Trials
programme.desc: Live coverage of the final day of the prestigious equestrian event at Badminton House in South Gloucestershire. The globally renowned trials feature dressage, cross-country and jumping across several days

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.sub-title: Warrington Wolves v Hull Kingston Rovers 10.05
programme.desc: Warrington Wolves v Hull Kingston Rovers (Kick-off 4.00pm). All the action from the second semi-final, held at Eco-Power Stadium in Doncaster. This is a repeat of last years final, which Hull KR edged 8-6 to win the trophy for the first time since 1980, before completing a treble by winning the League Leaders Shield and Super League. However, the Wolves have been in good form so far this term so will be hopeful of avenging that defeat

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Surprisingly few people have seen a badger in the wild and little is known about their private lives. Hidden cameras monitored a sett in Devon for more than a year, revealing previously unknown aspects of the creatures everyday existence. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce marks the 80th anniversary of VE Day in Berlin and Bletchley Park. Featuring interviews with veterans and the stories of those who helped liberate Europe, including a 100-year-old woman who worked alongside Allied codebreakers and a German army veteran who reflects on the rise of the Nazi regime. Items valued include a pistol taken from a German general, stationery from Hitlers mountain retreat, model tanks from a training school, and a pair of miniature clogs

programme.title: The Elon Musk Show
programme.desc: Using extensive archive footage taken throughout his life, from South Africa to Silicon Valley, the series analyses the key moments in the entrepreneurs career from his first tech start-ups through to Tesla and SpaceX. In 1995, at the start of the Silicon Valley tech boom, Musk began his ascendency, building and selling a major new internet technology business, becoming a multi-millionaire at the age of just 28 - but just as he was enjoying newfound success, he faced his first major challenge

programme.title: Scandinavia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer explores the hidden gems of the countries of Scandinavia, marvelling at the Arctic landscapes and spectacular scenery along the way. The first leg of his journey takes him from the archipelago that marks the northernmost outpost of Norway, Svalbard, to the snowy region of Lapland - travelling along Natos longest border with Russia where tensions are high and Santas village is prepared for a potential war

programme.title: Inside Britains National Parks
programme.desc: Steeped in myth and untamed beauty, Dartmoors vast moorlands are characterised by open heathland, grassy hills and striking granite tors, providing a sanctuary for rare plant and animal species, as well as a treasure trove for archaeologists. Rivers meandering through the moors are home to salmon, otters and kingfishers, and peatlands host rare plants like the carnivorous sundew

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week, with policy-makers questioned on the key issues

programme.title: The Iron Claw (2023)
programme.desc: Biographical sports drama about the Von Erich family, who made history in the intensely competitive world of professional wrestling in the early 1980s, but were beset by tragedy. Starring Zac Efron, Jeremy Allen White and Lily James

programme.title: Suzie Lees Great Food Made Simple
programme.desc: Suzie Lee Arbuthnot visits Artis Restaurant in Londonderry and is inspired to make a sticky toffee souffle

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an hour of post-election debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: Niall invites Ruben to join him and his flatmates for freshers week at university, but what begins with excitement ends in devastating consequences. Written by and starring Richard Gadd, with Jamie Bell

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts a quiz celebrating the UK and all its quirks, with Traitors star Paul Gorton acting as an unreliable guide leading a daily tour packed with fun, facts and fibs. The first weeks contestants are Anneka Rice, Shane Todd and Remi Burgz, with Whitby the subject of the opening heat

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey, Michael Portillo travels to South Koreas south west, where food, protest and landscape reveal the values that have shaped the modern nation. Arriving by high-speed train in Gwangju, he explores the regions deep-rooted food culture. Later, the presenter confronts a darker chapter in South Koreas history at the May 18th Memorial Park. Leaving the mainland, Michael crosses by ferry to Jeju Island, a volcanic landscape dominated by Mount Halla

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how a remote garden on Scotlands west coast has become an oasis for barn owls and pine martens as they battle the elements. During the brief summer, crossbills pry seeds from the cones in specially planted Scots pine trees, house martins raise their chicks, reptiles are finally warm enough to breed, and carnivorous sundews trap insects in a specially created bog garden

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Arsenal at London Stadium and Burnley v Aston Villa at Turf Moor. The relegation-threatened Hammers took on their title-chasing London rivals, and the Villans continued their bid to secure Champions League football against a Burnley side already resigned to playing in the Championship next term. Plus, Crystal Palace v Everton and Nottingham Forest v Newcastle United

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible

programme.title: Mastermind

They then get a chance to demonstrate the depth of their general knowledge

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The team is in west London to help the Parkinson family. With four young children, the house is overrun with toys and the hallway is a dumping ground

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: The annual flood arrives in the Okavango Delta, bringing new prey and challenges for the big cats. Gordon Buchanan follows the hungry lion mothers as they hunt buffalo, while eldest cub Mathata learns to avoid this formidable prey. Anna Dimitriadis tracks a skinny young cheetah struggling to catch food in the changing landscape and Brad Bestelink follows a pregnant leopard looking for a den as rising flood waters push dangerous males into her territory

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the team return to Royal Holloway college in Surrey, where items assessed include a belt clasp linking Annie Oakley and Queen Victoria, a ring with an unusual coloured diamond, two bargain jugs and a very rare gold watch

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin, Caroline Hawley and Mark Stacey head to Dartmoor, where items of interest include a platinum disc awarded for Coldplays 2005 album XandY, which is signed by the bands members. Also, an expert in America talks about a walking stick that is associated with General Tom Thumb

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Deirdre OKane, Jay Rayner, Tessa Sanderson and Iwan Thomas test their general knowledge skills in a variety of fun trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo follows the old Silk Road trade routes through Uzbekistan, starting in the capita Tashkent, where he takes in its vast, vibrant market and samples popular Uzbek delicacies. He travels on the lavishly decorated Soviet-era underground, which was hidden from the outside world until 2018, when a ban on photographing it was lifted. Michael hears how an earthquake resulted in Tashkent becoming the capital of Soviet modernist architecture, and he makes his TikTok debut with a globally famous social-media sta

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Andrew and dad Terrence are spinning plates on the farm and with their glamping pods just as Gayle is giving birth to the newest member of the Glenn family. Long days working on the dairy shed build and an infection in his leg mean Adam is desperate for a new prosthetic, while Ally and fellow crofters set sail to the volcanic Shiant islands, where they must navigate steep cliffs to find and shear sheep

programme.title: The Elon Musk Show
programme.desc: In 2009, the financial crash sent shockwaves across the world and threatened to ruin Elon Musk. The programme looks at how he tried to revolutionise the car market with the all-electric Tesla, while his rocket enterprise SpaceX was awarded a Nasa contract and started to enjoy success

programme.title: Children of the Blitz
programme.desc: The stories of some of the two million British children who were not evacuated during the Second World War, staying at home in towns and cities at risk of being bombed, often to help their families or because their parents couldnt bear to send them away. This documentary features some of the last people who were witness to the reality of the Blitz, with their testimonies offering an intimate, enlightening and moving portrait of wartime Britain through childrens eyes

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day, presented by Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: A Bigger Splash (2015)
programme.desc: A rock star recovering from surgery retreats to the peaceful surroundings of a Mediterranean island with her lover. Their seclusion is disrupted by the arrival of an old flame and his estranged teenage daughter, and a complex, tempestuous relationship develops between the four of them, bringing old memories to the surface. Drama, starring Tilda Swinton and Ralph Fiennes

programme.title: Iolos River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo Williams journeys through south Wales to explore the River Usk. His 77-mile journey begins near the industrial docklands of Newport, where the iconic transporter bridge stands as a gateway to surprising urban biodiversity. Moving upstream to the gravelly stretches near Usk town, the river becomes a vital spawning ground for rare shad, a sea fish that migrates to freshwater to lay their eggs

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Vick Hope explore the chalk landscapes of the Chilterns, where rare grasslands, seasonal streams and specialised wildlife all depend on the fragile ground. Matt meets farmers working together to reconnect these landscapes, while Vick visits a rare winterbourne stream that flows only after winter rains. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes a rare breed Middle White pig to his Cotswolds farm

The Soviet-era capital is the countrys cultural and financial hub, and to get there, the teams face the choice between two countries - north into Kazakhstan, travelling between major cities, or into Kyrgyzstan, a country rarely visited by western tourists, with remote valleys, alpine lakes and winding mountain roads

programme.sub-title: Yosemite National Park: Without a Trace

programme.desc: In 1980, security officers near the East Gate of RAF Woodbridg encountered a luminous craft and dancing red lights in Suffolk. Plus, the report of a metallic UFO hovering over water then crashing in Nova Scotia in 1967

programme.sub-title: Men in Black and Room with a View

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: Recounting an instance when an Australian rancher went to check what was frightening his herd, only to spot a large UFO hovering above a water tank. The object then sped off into the distance

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: Recounting how members of a Christian youth group observed a falling star while on a hillside. Things became even more unusual when an object landed close to them

programme.desc: Tensions mount when a man loses his temper over a mix-up with his gold chain. Meanwhile, an allegedly cursed relic scares Ashley

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: An employee shocks the family by turning violent, and Ashley loses her temper when someone tries to steal her underwear

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Seth and his father clash when Less idea turns dangerous, and Ashley embarks on a new in-house business venture

programme.desc: Staff fight over whether they should buy a batch of venomous reptiles, and an incident forces Les to seek a new head of security

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: The Way of the Gam Gam

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Lisa comes to Montebello with a clear mind, intent on letting the lockers speak to her. Ivy lets his son navigate his way to a unit which turns out not to be completely vacant. Kenny is absolutely appalled by what he finds abandoned in his unit

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Ivy drags Isaiah to Victorville as punishment for getting Cs on his report card. Kenny wants to furnish his new house, but there are eight reasons standing in his way. Elsewhere, Lisa learns a lot

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Brandi is fired up about expanding her brand in San Bernardino. Rene is positive that everyone is out to get him. Dusty tackles a locker dustier than him. Kenny is a mess

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Actor Laurence Fishburne explores the legend of Bigfoot, the legendary North American creature that has never been officially identified. As evidence continues to mount and photos are scrutinised, the mystery endures - does Bigfoot really exist?

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: When the team flies a cutting-edge radar device high above Skinwalker Ranch, it reveals shocking new clues about the Bubble and what may be inside the Mesa

programme.title: Hazardous History with Henry Winkler
programme.desc: The actor explores the thrilling world of vintage daredevils, from the human fly to skateboarding speedsters and diving horses. These bold risk-takers chased adrenaline with unforgettable, dangerous stunts

programme.title: The Witches of Salem
programme.desc: The Salem witch trials began with the case against Bridget Bishop, who is quickly found guilty and sentenced to death by hanging. Her death was not enough to quell the hysteria, and five more women were tried and executed within a month. Narrated by Mark Strong

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring anomalies appearing on satellite images to try and get a better understanding of unexplained phenomena. This episode features the haunting history of the town that time forgot, unexplained circles in the New Mexico desert and a possible clue to extra-terrestrial life in the Arctic

programme.title: Legends of the Pharaohs
programme.sub-title: Tutankhamun, The Pharaoh with the Fabulous Treasure
programme.desc: A look at how the discovery of Tutankhamuns largely intact tomb in 1922 was arguably more significant than his short reign as a pharaoh. He was buried in a golden coffin surrounded by 5,000 priceless treasures, including animal statues, jewellery, clothes, weapons and even toys

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Swamp Beast Sighting, Martian Conspiracy and Transparent Mexican Sea Monster
programme.desc: An investigation into a remarkable sighting of the skunk ape, a creature often likened to Bigfoot. A canoer claims to have captured footage of this elusive beast navigating through gator-infested waters, raising questions about what may be lurking in the swamps. Plus, the discovery of a peculiar transparent sea monster off the coast of Mexico, which has sparked the curiosity of marine enthusiasts and experts alike

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Modern Day Dinosaur Sighting, Mysterious Lights Above Norway, and Girl Punches Down Tree
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things caught on film. This episodes features reports of a dinosaur living in the African Congo, strange lights spotted above the Hessdalen Valley in Norway, a 12-year-old girl in Russia with superhuman strength and proof of an alien structure on an asteroid

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Rubber Duck UFO, Grim Reaper Ice Sculpture, and I Killed Bigfoot
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things caught on film. This episode features leaked video of a UFO shaped like a rubber duck, a man in India able to dip his hands in boiling oil and feel no pain, the overnight appearance of the Grim Reaper sculpted in ice, and a man who claims to have killed Bigfoot

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts reveals the history of Belfast, the UKs most Victorian city. She visits the shipyards that built the Titanic, gets locked up in the notorious Crumlin Road gaol and takes a crash course in 19th-century female etiquette. The final episode of this unique take on British history, in which the academic, writer and broadcaster studies one key period in history by delving into the secrets of a historic town that encapsulates the era, providing an impression of what life was really like at key moments in the turbulent past

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the Second World War history of Dover, visiting an underground base beneath the famous white cliffs, from where the Dunkirk evacuation was masterminded. She also talks gets a first-hand account of the towns wartime history from a 90-year-old local and experiences what life was like for members of the Auxiliary Territorial Service during the Battle of Britain. Plus, aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson takes to the sky to survey the challenges faced by Royal Navy vessels deployed in the English Channel
