programme.desc: A hot-air balloon carrying five passengers is swept off course and crashes on an uncharted island, becoming the adventure of a lifetime as months turn into years. From the classic novel by Jules Verne

programme.title: Jules Vernes Mysterious Island
programme.desc: A hot-air balloon carrying five passengers is swept off course and crashes on an uncharted island, becoming the adventure of a lifetime as months turn into years. From the classic novel by Jules Verne

programme.title: The Twilight Zone
programme.sub-title: Nervous Man in a Four-Dollar Room
programme.desc: Small-time crook Jackie Rhoades finds he has company when his reflection starts talking to him. Presented by Rod Serling

programme.title: The Twilight Zone
programme.desc: A bad-tempered writers electric typewriter develops a hold over him. Presented by Rod Serling

programme.title: Oldboy (2013)
programme.desc: A man is kidnapped and held hostage for 20 years, until one day he is mysteriously released. Determined to avenge himself on his captors, he investigates a web of sinister conspiracies to discover who it was that imprisoned him and why. Spike Lees remake of the Korean thriller, starring Josh Brolin, Elizabeth Olsen and Samuel L Jackson

programme.title: Riddick (2013)
programme.desc: A fugitive is left for dead by his treacherous followers on a dangerous world full of lethal predators. He lures bounty hunters to the planet in the hope of killing them and stealing their spacecraft, but finds himself forced to work with them against an attack by the monsters at the top of the food chain. Sci-fi thriller sequel, starring Vin Diesel and Katee Sackhoff

programme.title: Fortress (2021)
programme.desc: When his estranged son comes to visit him in the woods, a retired CIA agent realises an old enemy is following him. Action thriller, starring Bruce Willis, Jesse Metcalfe and Chad Michael Murray

programme.title: Interview with a Hitman (2012)
programme.desc: An assassin is raised to be the perfect killing machine in the slums of Romania, but when his masters betray him, he fakes his death and tries to start a new life in London. However, he soon finds his dark past coming back to haunt him. Action thriller, starring Luke Goss, Caroline Tillette and Danny Midwinter

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The lawman attempts to thwart a white supremacist gangs plan to trigger bombs during a racial harmony rally. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: The veteran policeman has just a phonebook and a key as clues when the body of a girl is washed up on the beach, and is later attacked by the prime suspect for her murder

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: Steve is imprisoned while trying to clear his boss of smuggling charges. Farley Granger guest stars

programme.title: Geo-Disaster (2017)
programme.desc: A family in Los Angeles finds itself separated during a convergence of historys greatest disasters - a super volcano, a mega earthquake and a massive twister. Sci-fi drama, starring Matthew Pohlkamp, Natalie Pelletier and Erich Riegelmann

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: Dakota Incident (1956)
programme.desc: Mismatched travellers, including a bank robber, a politician and a dancing girl, are targeted by Cheyenne braves when their stagecoach is ambushed in the Dakota desert. When the coach is set on fire, they take cover in a gully, and a drawn-out conflict ensues. Western, starring Dale Robertson, Linda Darnell, John Lund and Ward Bond

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: Jory (1972)
programme.desc: Western, chronicling the difficult transition to manhood of a teenager whose father and friends are killed in a saloon brawl. John Marley, BJ Thomas and Robby Benson star

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: Captain Kirk is baffled by the indifference of the planet Organias rulers to his warnings of an imminent Klingon invasion - and ends up trapped behind enemy lines. William Shatner, Leonard Nimoy and John Colicos star

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: The vehicle Steve Austin was piloting when he had his crash has been re-built, and he is asked to pilot it by his old friend. Clifton James guest stars

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: Romano and Hooker suspect a vigilante group is killing criminals freed by the courts on technicalities, and set about luring its members into a trap. Vintage American drama, starring William Shatner and Adrian Zmed

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The lawman demands justice when his martial arts teachers son is murdered, but the gang have a secret deadly weapon. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: Mechanic: Resurrection (2016)
programme.desc: Hitman Arthur Bishops arch-enemy Crain kidnaps his new girlfriend Gina and blackmails him into committing three assassinations in as many days, while making them look like accidents. Action thriller sequel, starring Jason Statham, Jessica Alba, Sam Hazeldine and Tommy Lee Jones

programme.title: Machete (2010)
programme.desc: A Mexican cop-turned-mercenary is hired by a shadowy businessman to assassinate a politician. When he realises his employers have double-crossed him, he joins forces with a band of militant illegal immigrants to seek a suitably bloody revenge. Action thriller, starring Danny Trejo, Robert De Niro and Jessica Alba

programme.title: Machete Kills (2013)
programme.desc: The relentless Mexican mercenary is hired by the president of the United States for a vitally important mission. A billionaire arms dealer intends to start a nuclear war using a space-based weapon of mass destruction, but defeating his schemes involves a deadly encounter with a murderous master of disguise. Action thriller sequel, starring Danny Trejo and Mel Gibson

programme.title: Standoff (2015)
programme.desc: A troubled former soldier who is haunted by his past actions comes to the aid of a 12-year-old girl who has witnessed a murder. He vows to protect her from the assassin pursuing her, but is forced to use unconventional methods to keep the killer at bay when he realises his gun only has one bullet. Thriller, starring Thomas Jane and Laurence Fishburne

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Medics at St Georges hospital treat three women who have been stabbed in a Surrey supermarket car park in broad daylight. Meanwhile, friends and relations recall their associations with the wounded, Jean, 67, Rani, 71, and Janet, 62

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics race against the clock as a man shows signs of a stroke. A drunk man is stranded in the street and has to be taken to hospital for his own safety. A factory worker is in agony with severe back pain and very high blood pressure. Plus, a man has a close call when he thinks he is having a heart attack

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Lewis, 18, is rushed into the hospital after coming off his motorbike and colliding with two cars. He is immediately taken for a full body CT scan as doctors are concerned about the extent of the injuries to his pelvis, arm and head. Meanwhile, 27-year-old Sam awaits X-ray results after he fainted and cut his lip while smoking a Cuban cigar at a barbecue

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Headteacher Emma arrives in resus having been in a collision with a bus while cycling home from the school she works at. As she is treated, her husband Mick reflects on being informed what had happened. Meanwhile, 33-year-old Josh is treated for severe back pain, and 87-year-old Pamela is examined for a possible blood clot in her leg, and tells the doctors about her childhood in London during the Blitz

programme.title: Supermarkets Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team head for the Vegan Aisle for all the news on animal-free food. Helen Lawal visits Tesco to find out how they keep up with all the plant-based burger trend, John Whaite seeks out the secret to the snap in a vegan sausage skin, Kate Quilton finds out how exactly you milk an oat, and Briony May Williams learns how to make a quiche without egg

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This weeks competition is in and around Leeds, where the first host, 38-year-old foster carer and nature lover Shaun, is serving up an Asia-meets-Yorkshire fusion menu complete with dumplings, Chinese fish and chips and a not-so-traditional Yorkshire pudding

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Aspiring model Ryan is hoping that his French and Caribbean cuisine will tantalise his guests tastebuds in Leeds. During the drinks reception, Ryan is fuming when one guest flouts his runway-ready dress code, and at the table, Ryan bites back with his egg salad bombs then insults another diner when they comment on his clobber. Desperate to lighten the mood, he hosts a modelling masterclass, but his controversial views on beauty backfire. Having spent the evening insulting and then bickering with his guests, has Ryan blown his chances to bag the prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On night three in Leeds, medical trainer Jen hosts a pink-themed party in an attempt to bag the big bucks. Despite her overuse of pink food colouring, her guests are impressed with her home-cooked dishes, but when Shaun questions her on their provenance, shes forced to come clean. Philip teases the table with his hidden pink side and Ryan isnt afraid to throw some insults when Philip reveals that he won a clothing competition. A surprise performance in the garden proves a cut above for hairdresser Sarah, but will it be enough for Jen to grab the grand?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the penultimate night in Leeds, a Middle Eastern mezze is the suggested offering from freelance creative Philip. Shaun is impressed with his homemade sourdough, but a cheesecake doesnt cut it for hairdresser Sarah. The group gets creative with some clay modelling, but it all goes pear-shaped when Ryan continues to throw insults at his fellow guests. Jen entertains the table with a bizarre cleaning story and Philip ventures into the woods for a muddy mis-endeavour

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night in Leeds, hair salon owner Sarah hopes to delight her diners with some classic 1980s cuisine. With a reputation for being outspoken, Sarah gets a dose of her own medicine from one diner whos determined to get their own back. Later, a guest reveals a freaky phobia and theres a hair-raising horror story before some very fancy footwork on the dancefloor. Will an evening of disco dancing and retro grub be enough for Sarah to take top spot?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Chef Mark and yoga teacher Loretta, who live in west London, have been together for 15 years after meeting in Lorettas native Australia. Theyre looking to find their perfect property near Estepona on Spains Costa del Sol for their budget of £200,000. Craig Rowe lines up five options for the pair to choose between

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps Sheffield couple Rob and Lisa find a three-bedroom holiday home with a swimming pool in the Lanzarote resort of Playa Blanca on a budget of £280,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Plumber Richard and music composer Chris, who has a visual impairment, met 10 years ago and bonded as friends after Richard agreed to become Chris tour guide on holiday. The pair now go on regular getaways and the south coast of Tenerife has become a firm favourite, so much so that theyve decided to join forces to buy their own property there as a winter bolthole. With a combined budget of £110,000, they task Ben Hillman to find them the perfect pad

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Simon and Lawrence have been visiting the peaceful Charente region of south-west France for nearly 20 years and know the area inside out. Now, theyre looking for a holiday home there for an ideal budget of £100,000. Theyre after a traditional-style property with enough storage space for their mountain bikes, but Jasmine Harman could be facing an uphill struggle to impress such a knowledgeable couple

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Kathryn and Mark find a home on the Kassandra Peninsula in the north of Greece. The pair, now approaching retirement, want plenty of room for the family - and their dog - and be near to a beach. They have a budget of £80,000 to invest in the move

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil catch up with Catherine and Nigel four years after their first visit when they helped them decide what to do with their home in Larne, Northern Ireland. Catherine wanted to move from the disjointed small roomed bungalow in an isolated location but Nigel, who didnt want to buy the property in the first place, was in love with their large garden with stunning views. While Phil took the pair to see some impressive but affordable houses, Kirstie came up with a plan to repurpose their space. Now theyre back to see if the pair stuck to their decision

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: The architect tries to unlock the potential of old properties. He starts out in Stroud, where Phil and Leighla are transforming their ramshackle, listed Georgian farmhouse with an industrial-style extension. George also pays a visit to Wootton Bassett, where he helps a couple flip the layout of their Victorian end-of-terrace home

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Redmire Village Pub in North Yorkshire, where hosts Kerrie and Vickie hope the cosy vibes and stunning countryside setting will be a hit

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week comes from The Yorkshire Hosts in historic Harrogate, where Hollie and Ben reckon their plush, self-catering apartments in the heart of the town centre will go down a storm - but the lack of free parking proves problematic, leaving the hosts frustrated

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Tabitha and Steve host the third visit of the competition at Copper Beech Glamping in Staffordshire. They have a relax and recharge ethos at their glampsite, with a tranquil rural setting and luxurious outdoor hot tubs, but unfortunately, one BandBer in particular isnt so relaxed about the idea of the great outdoors

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Berkshire, where Ana and Dominic hope their homely pub, the Bull Inn, will charm the BandBers with its quintessential character. First impressions are promising and the hosts hope to finish on a high with a barnstorming breakfast. However talk of the looming payment day raises tensions

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment day promises to be a spicy affair, with high emotions, awkward exchanges and plenty of pernickety comments to unpick. As the dust settles, the weeks winner emerges

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: Dr David and nurse Ebony urgently check a young motorcyclist for internal bleeding and spinal damage after he collided with a car. A mans foot has been mangled in a forklift accident. And an 81-year-old grandmother cant remember flipping her car

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 79-year-old man arrives in hospital after collapsing in the street whilst walking his dog, and doctors give him medication to try and stabilise his very high heart rate. Meanwhile, a young boy is treated after pushing a plastic toy down his ear, and doctors show concern for a man who has numbness in his arm and little finger, and send him for a CT scan

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Marie, 31, arrives at St Georges AandE in south-west London by ambulance after collapsing at home with an unexplained headache. Doctors work fast to ascertain the cause of her collapse, with potentially devastating results. Maries joined by her partner Cameron and her young child. Cameras also follow Eileen, 87, who comes in with shortness of breath and a high heart rate, and 10-month-old Erin, who has a build-up of wax in her ears following a cold and fever

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: Dr David and nurse Ebony urgently check a young motorcyclist for internal bleeding and spinal damage after he collided with a car. A mans foot has been mangled in a forklift accident. And an 81-year-old grandmother cant remember flipping her car

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 79-year-old man arrives in hospital after collapsing in the street whilst walking his dog, and doctors give him medication to try and stabilise his very high heart rate. Meanwhile, a young boy is treated after pushing a plastic toy down his ear, and doctors show concern for a man who has numbness in his arm and little finger, and send him for a CT scan

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Medics at St Georges hospital treat three women who have been stabbed in a Surrey supermarket car park in broad daylight. Meanwhile, friends and relations recall their associations with the wounded, Jean, 67, Rani, 71, and Janet, 62

programme.title: Supermarkets Unwrapped
programme.desc: The Food Unwrapped team take a deep dive into the nations favourite supermarket aisles. In this first episode, the team chill out in the freezer aisle. Jimmy Doherty investigates why theres more to frozen chips than just potatoes, Helen Lawal learns why frozen ready meals have best-before dates, Briony May Williams gets the inside scoop on soft-scoop ice cream, and Andi Oliver learns how to get the most out of your freezer at home

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien converts tank parts to garden furniture, while Henry Cole takes his life in his hands with a motorised skateboard. Later, Simon restores a model Hurricane, and Henry goes potty for a retro petrol pump

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to a private railway, where Simon converts a lantern to a stylish table and Henry turns a fire extinguisher into a steampunk lamp

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at The Admiral Hotel in central London, run by owner So Shaw and hotel manager Kevin Jankunas. Chris Foster and his German companion Christel Richter are floored by the London prices, and Ursula Beswick and Daria Pakalnicka question the quality of the facilities. Despite So and Kevins best efforts to charm their guests, a dinner conversation leaves Kris Malde and Vikki Cheatham making accusations of sexism and So and Kevin are outraged by some of their feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to The Redstone in Blackpool, where high-end hotelier Ursula Beswick and manager Daria Pakalnicka hope their luxury guest house will win the day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Chris Foster and Christel Richter invite the hoteliers to Anns Farmhouse in the suburbs of Bradford, West Yorkshire, but tensions remain high between So, Kevin, Kris and Vikki

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to the R Inn in Desborough, Northamptonshire. The establishment is owned by entrepreneur Kris Malde, whos helped by events manager Vikki Cheetham. Kris opened the hotel to add corporate-style rooms for his events space next door. Ursula and Daria think the facilities are lacking and when the simmering tensions between So and Kevin and Kris and Vikki boil over at dinner, So refuses to stay the night. Chris and Christel reject the coffee at breakfast, and Kris and Vikki face some tough feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: As the BandB owners meet for the last time, bones of contention include a missing biscuit, marks on a toilet seat and one guest is confronted about why they refused to stay the night. There are harsh words and home truths before the overall winner is revealed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hospital theatre practitioner Geoff Conway holds the first dinner party in Wigan, hoping his nine-course Spanish tapas feast will impress his fellow diners and secure him the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Outgoing hair salon owner Natalie Crompton hosts the second dinner party in Wigan, and since she loves to party she plans a fun-filled night for her guests - including some very special treats

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Music teacher and funeral singer Alan Gregory hosts the third dinner party in Wigan, and lines up an evening of Greek food for his guests. However, his plans for a cultured soiree are soon spoiled by straight-talking Geoff Conway, who seems intent on causing arguments

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Gymnastics teacher Simon Grimwood is hoping to host a special evening on the fourth night of the contest in Wigan, Greater Manchester, because it is his birthday. He wants his posh menu to dazzle his guests, but salon owner Natalie Crompton is worried about some of the food on offer. The other contestants also discover his secret lifelong passion for ferreting

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nurse practitioner Caroline Heaven hosts the final night in Wigan, hoping to end the week by showing her guests a proper Lancashire night. However, hospital theatre manager Geoff Conway is on the verge of being thrown out if he continues his run of misbehaviour. At the end of proceedings the winner of the £1,000 is revealed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Callum Hogg and Lauren Abrahams modern Park Hotel in Gloucestershire, where sisters Sue Lewis and Wendy Germann are impressed by the colourful decor, but its not an opinion held by all. The guests are not impressed with having to pay extra for breakfast and Callum and Lauren are faced with robust feedback that leaves them wanting answers and thinking not everyone is being fair

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Trowbridge in Wiltshire to Sues BandB, which is run by Sue Lewis and her sister, Wendy Germann. Breakfast at the communal table has its awkward moments and Sue is under pressure, having never cooked for so many before. However, its the feedback that leaves the hosts wondering if maybe some of the guests have not been that straightforward

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to The Talbot in Iwerne Minster, Dorset, for the third day of the competition, where hosts Erika Watt and Ellie Gill greet their guests. The rooms do not live up to their good first impression and Callum and Lauren are quick to find fault. At breakfast, tensions simmer as Wendy calls Cameron a creep, and at feedback, some of the comments send Erika and Ellie into fits of giggles

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth visit of the week is to The Old Rectory in Llangattock, south Wales, where young hotel manager Cameron Thomas likes to keep things professional. Despite wanting to find fault, Lauren and Callum are soon to admit their bridal suite is top notch, but Erika and Ellie think there may be some cleaning issues. During an afternoon of canoe racing the group members show their competitive sides

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guest house owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. The Park Hotel in Gloucestershire is up first as owners Callum Hogg and Lauren Abraham want to get to the bottom of why Cameron didnt sleep soundly. Next, Sue Lewis from Sues BandB seeks to discover the deasons for Callum and Laurens low score for the cleanliness. When all issues are resolved, the winning establishment is revealed

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go househunting in the West Yorkshire city of Leeds. Kinga and Dave are moving from London with their toddler Lily, to get closer to Daves northern roots. However, they have already lost out on offers in the vibrant market and are hoping Phil can come up with the goods. Kirstie is with Max, who is leaving Birmingham to be closer to his new partner Dan. However, it quickly becomes clear he may be expecting too much for his budget

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet Emily and Kris in the village of Somersham, Cambridgeshire, who are unsure whether to sell their three-bed semi or renovate instead. Primary school teacher Emily thinks they need a bigger home with more space for their growing children, but Kris feels their home has too much potential and would prefer to stay put

programme.title: Our Tiny Islands
programme.desc: The custodians of Station Island, on County Donegals Lough Derg, welcome over 10,000 people each year for a gruelling three-day pilgrimage without food or shoes. Roughly 150 miles to the north, Iona shares Stations monastic tradition. But life there is very different for Ailidh, who runs the islands unique takeaway

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The staff have a mystery to unravel when an unknown patient arrives after collapsing in the street. The team eventually manage to contact his daughters as they try to find out what may have caused him to fall unconscious. Meanwhile, a 29-year-old man comes in following an accident at the gym, and a three-year-old girl is in paediatrics with her mum showing signs of a chest infection

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A man involved in a car crash needs to be carefully removed from his vehicle to protect his spine. Its also a day when everyday errands go badly wrong. A slip on fallen crab apples in the street leaves a woman in agony with a suspected broken hip. An afternoon spent pottering in the garden ends with a fall headfirst into a brick wall. A bicycle leaves a young man with a swollen kneecap that paramedics suspect is broken, while picking up a prescription from the chemist ends with a collapse and a trip to hospital

programme.desc: The custodians of Station Island, on County Donegals Lough Derg, welcome over 10,000 people each year for a gruelling three-day pilgrimage without food or shoes. The pair now go on regular getaways and the south coast of Tenerife has become a firm favourite, so much so that theyve decided to join forces to buy their own property there as a winter bolthole. With a combined budget of £110,000, they task Ben Hillman to find them the perfect pad

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Simon and Lawrence have been visiting the peaceful Charente region of south-west France for nearly 20 years and know the area inside out. Now, theyre looking for a holiday home there for an ideal budget of £100,000. Theyre after a traditional-style property with enough storage space for their mountain bikes, but Jasmine Harman could be facing an uphill struggle to impress such a knowledgeable couple

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Kathryn and Mark find a home on the Kassandra Peninsula in the north of Greece. The pair, now approaching retirement, want plenty of room for the family - and their dog - and be near to a beach. They have a budget of £80,000 to invest in the move

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil catch up with Catherine and Nigel four years after their first visit when they helped them decide what to do with their home in Larne, Northern Ireland. Catherine wanted to move from the disjointed small roomed bungalow in an isolated location but Nigel, who didnt want to buy the property in the first place, was in love with their large garden with stunning views. While Phil took the pair to see some impressive but affordable houses, Kirstie came up with a plan to repurpose their space. Now theyre back to see if the pair stuck to their decision

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: The architect tries to unlock the potential of old properties. He starts out in Stroud, where Phil and Leighla are transforming their ramshackle, listed Georgian farmhouse with an industrial-style extension. George also pays a visit to Wootton Bassett, where he helps a couple flip the layout of their Victorian end-of-terrace home

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Redmire Village Pub in North Yorkshire, where hosts Kerrie and Vickie hope the cosy vibes and stunning countryside setting will be a hit

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week comes from The Yorkshire Hosts in historic Harrogate, where Hollie and Ben reckon their plush, self-catering apartments in the heart of the town centre will go down a storm - but the lack of free parking proves problematic, leaving the hosts frustrated

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Berkshire, where Ana and Dominic hope their homely pub, the Bull Inn, will charm the BandBers with its quintessential character. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.desc: Dr David and nurse Ebony urgently check a young motorcyclist for internal bleeding and spinal damage after he collided with a car. A mans foot has been mangled in a forklift accident. And an 81-year-old grandmother cant remember flipping her car

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 79-year-old man arrives in hospital after collapsing in the street whilst walking his dog, and doctors give him medication to try and stabilise his very high heart rate. Meanwhile, a young boy is treated after pushing a plastic toy down his ear, and doctors show concern for a man who has numbness in his arm and little finger, and send him for a CT scan

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hospital theatre practitioner Geoff Conway holds the first dinner party in Wigan, hoping his nine-course Spanish tapas feast will impress his fellow diners and secure him the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Outgoing hair salon owner Natalie Crompton hosts the second dinner party in Wigan, and since she loves to party she plans a fun-filled night for her guests - including some very special treats

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Music teacher and funeral singer Alan Gregory hosts the third dinner party in Wigan, and lines up an evening of Greek food for his guests. However, his plans for a cultured soiree are soon spoiled by straight-talking Geoff Conway, who seems intent on causing arguments

programme.desc: Part one of two. Nazi Germanys invasion of the Soviet Union. Launched in June 1941, it was one of the largest military offensives in history, involving millions of Axis soldiers and marking a significant turning-point in the Second World War. The invasion was a surprise to the Soviets, despite significant troop build-up at the border. In the first hours of the attack, bombs rained down on Soviet airfields

programme.desc: Highlighting the actions of the diplomats in Berlin who tried to point out the dangers and reality of a Nazi Germany who went perilously ignored after Hitler was appointed chancellor in 1933

programme.title: WWII: Operation Barbarossa
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nazi Germanys invasion of the Soviet Union. Launched in June 1941, it was one of the largest military offensives in history, involving millions of Axis soldiers and marking a significant turning-point in the Second World War. The invasion was a surprise to the Soviets, despite significant troop build-up at the border. In the first hours of the attack, bombs rained down on Soviet airfields

programme.title: WWII: Operation Barbarossa
programme.desc: Part two of two. Determined to seize the Russian capital before winter, Hitler launched Operation Typhoon. It was to be the last great decisive battle of 1941, and one that would seal the fate of Eastern Europe

programme.title: Pearl Harbor: The Heroes Who Fought Back
programme.desc: Examining the events of the attack on Pearl Harbor and the impact it had on how America engaged with war for the next 75 years along with a highlight on the heroic acts of ordinary men. Using a minute-by-minute timeline and the PhotoG technique, this documentary gives a definitive account of the pivotal day

programme.title: Ambassadors in Berlin: The Whistleblowers of WWII
programme.desc: Highlighting the actions of the diplomats in Berlin who tried to point out the dangers and reality of a Nazi Germany who went perilously ignored after Hitler was appointed chancellor in 1933

programme.title: The Wheelchair President
programme.desc: Part one of two. Examining the leadership of American president Franklin Roosevelt, historian David Reynolds exposes the cover-up of his failing health and explores how that impacted on the Second World War?s endgame and his gruelling negotiations with Stalin and Churchill

programme.title: Weathered: Earths Extremes
programme.sub-title: Are We at the Tipping Point?
programme.desc: Maiya May journeys across the United States to explore climate tipping points. Discovering how critical climate systems are for the planet?s stability, and learning the science behind these changes

programme.title: Weathered: Earths Extremes
programme.desc: Maiya May visits the Isle de Jean Charles, Louisiana, one of the first communities in the United States to undergo a federally funded relocation due to climate change. She also learns about hurricanes from the Hurricane Hunters

programme.title: Secrets of the Royal Palaces
programme.desc: Kate Williams explores the history of Sandringham in Norfolk, which is one of the few royal residences owned personally by the Windsors. It was purchased in 1861 for the rakish Prince Edward in the hopes he would settle down in the countryside, but instead he turned it into the perfect party palace, with a bowling alley, billiards and endless hunting and shooting. The historian also reveals how Prince Philip once proposed demolishing the place to make way for a more modern replacement, before being forced to relent by the Queen Mother

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett travels from Britain to Jerusalem via Sicily, examining the expansion and unchecked ambition of the Norman empire, and how its impact still resonates in todays culture and politics. He discovers how, under the leadership of Scandinavian raider Rollo, these descendants of the Vikings first established themselves in northern France and became an unstoppable force of Christian knights and warriors

programme.title: The Wheelchair President
programme.desc: Part one of two. Examining the leadership of American president Franklin Roosevelt, historian David Reynolds exposes the cover-up of his failing health and explores how that impacted on the Second World War?s endgame and his gruelling negotiations with Stalin and Churchill

programme.title: The Rosenbergs: Atomic Spies
programme.desc: How, in 1950 with America in the throes of the Red Scare, seemingly ordinary couple Julius and Ethel Rosenberg found themselves the target of anti-Communist hysteria. Arrested by the FBI and accused of passing secret information to the USSR, they maintained their innocence while the media and public opinion seemed to condemned them. Their trial did nothing to change this and ended in a death sentence. Thirty years later, declassified files revealed that Ethel was not a spy, though Julius did commit espionage - primarily as a recruiter. Using archival footage, this documentary revisits the intriguing case

programme.title: National Theatre Live: Much Ado About Nothing
programme.desc: When a hotel owners daughter weds a soldier, not all guests are in the mood for love. A 2022 performance of Shakespeares comedy, starring Katherine Parkinson and John Heffernan

programme.title: Larry Graham and Graham Central Station
programme.desc: A 2010 concert by the pioneering funk bassist and his band at the Bataclan in Paris

programme.title: Record On
programme.desc: The stories behind classic albums, focusing on Paul Wellers journey through changing times in the 1990s, his new direction in sound and his resulting 1993 album Wild Wood

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.sub-title: Classic Albums: American Pie - Don McLean
programme.desc: Crowned by its titular overture, Don McLeans 1971 release American Pie is a classic of the folk-rock genre. Here, the singer discusses the intricacies of his songs, the sometimes fraught recording process, and the albums legacy. Other contributors include producer Ed Freeman and musician Jake Bugg, whose musical path was initiated when hearing Vincent for the first time on the TV. Plus, a poignant archive performance of George Michael performing The Grave

programme.title: Classic FM Live: A Film Music Spectacular
programme.desc: The Bournemouth Symphony Orchestra and the Ukrainian conductor Kirill Karabits perform popular film themes from the Royal Albert Hall including Star Wars and Jurassic Park

programme.title: Artist of the Year: Masterclass
programme.desc: Live tutorials on aspects of painting given by Portrait Artist of the Year and Landscape Artist of the Year contestants. This instalment focuses on pastel on stencil paper

programme.title: Artist of the Year: Masterclass
programme.desc: A tutorial on perspective in urban landscape, given by Portrait Artist of the Year and Landscape Artist of the Year contestants

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: The fate of a newlywed seems sealed after he suffers a string of accidents similar to his wifes first husband

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Jack and Ednas parrot lays a strange egg, but when the chick hatches it appears to be watching them

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A woman refuses to let her husband squander a $133,000 sweepstakes ticket inherited from her first dead spouse, so he plans on killing her. With Steve Forrest and Joanna Moore

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: Jack kills Phylliss husband and cashes in by marrying the widow, but after identifying her lost crocodile dressing case, he might have exposed himself. With Denholm Elliott and Hazel Court

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: The life and career of the English actor, writer, film-maker and raconteur, who won Academy Awards for his roles in Spartacus and Topkapi

programme.title: Andre Rieu
programme.desc: Dutch violin maestro Andre Rieu hosts a very special wedding party, thrilling the crowd with a host of celebratory songs

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.sub-title: I Like It Here in Wilmington
programme.desc: A troubled businessman contemplates murdering his wife so he can get his hands on her trust fund

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Two villains decide to take advantage of an old man

programme.title: Musical Masterpieces
programme.desc: Myleene Klass and Errollyn Wallen explore The Four Seasons, Antonio Vivaldis set of violin concertos. Features a special performance from Sinfonia Cymru.

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: Metallica members James Hetfield, Lars Ulrich, Kirk Hammett and Jason Newsted discuss the making of their 1991 self-titled LP, which became known among fans as the Black Album and included the hit singles Enter Sandman, The Unforgiven and Nothing Else Matters

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the English actor, who rose to fame on the stage and became a worldwide star with his roles in the Lord of the Rings and X-Men film franchises

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Actor Trevor Eve, Fortitude star Parminder Nagra and author Ben Okri are the celebrity sitters this week as a new group of portrait artists attempts to impress the judges

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: An unhappy man decides to give up on his boring marriage - but his wife seems unwilling to co-operate

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: An ambitious politician turns vigilante in an effort to crack down on violence

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: William Botibol is a compulsive gambler. While on a cruise he enters a betting pool to guess the number of miles the ship will travel in the course of 24 hours. William thinks he has figured out a way to guarantee he will win the bet. With Keenan Wynn and Fay Wray

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: Concern mounts over a bank tellers finances, but he and his brother think they have a foolproof plan to secure funds for the future. With Robert H Harris and Joanne Linville

programme.title: Andre Rieu: Welcome to My World
programme.desc: The violinist presents highlights of his worldwide tour, including performances of Radetzky March and Ave Maria

programme.title: Jean-Michel Jarre Live in Sevilla
programme.desc: A 2025 concert by the French electronic music pioneer in the Spanish citys Plaza de España, featuring performances of Oxygene, Equinoxe, Robots Dont Cry and Stardust

programme.title: Len Phillips Swing Orchestras 100 Years of Big Bands
programme.desc: Part one of two. Concert from Londons Cadogan Hall tracing the history of jazz, swing and big band music, with singers Matt Ford and Nicola Emmanuelle joining the orchestra under musical director Joe Pettitt

programme.title: Nothing Compares (2022)
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of Sinead OConnors rise to worldwide fame, and how her iconoclastic personality resulted in her exile from the pop mainstream

programme.title: Comedy Legends
programme.desc: Barry Cryer pays tribute to actor Peter Sellers, who shot to fame in The Goons and starred in the Pink Panther franchise and was Oscar-nominated for Dr Strangelove and Being There

programme.title: Mary Cassatt: Painting the Modern Woman
programme.desc: Revisit the career of Mary Cassatt through prints, pastels and paintings, exploring the often-overlooked impressionists influence on how women appear in art

programme.title: Atomic
programme.desc: In Beirut, deep cover CIA agent Cassie Elliott receives a tip off about an exchange of uranium that has apparently just taken place in the city, close to where Max and JJ are now laying low, and decides to investigate. Meanwhile, Max receives evidence that the cartel has kidnapped his girlfriend Laetitia and he must take the statues all the way to Marrakech if he is to save her

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Part one of two. A sniper strikes terror into the hearts of the residents of Baltimore. Gritty police drama, guest starring chat show host Jay Leno

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Part two of two. The officers continue their investigation into the sniper attacks and a copycat killer emerges. Gritty police drama, guest starring chat show host Jay Leno

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Kellerman and Lewis are ordered to Pennsylvania to extradite a female murder suspect. Police drama, starring Clark Johnson and Reed Diamond

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming visits Liverpool, cutting through cliches about football and the Beatles to explore its less-celebrated treasures. Among his destinations are a 1930s banking building that has remained unaltered since closing in 2007, a well-appointed set of toilets and a recording studio hidden in a bar. He also discovers which former dictator once spent Christmas in the city

programme.title: Sort Of
programme.desc: Imran makes a surprise drop-in on Sabi. Meanwhile, Bessy and Paul discover Henry is skipping school, and 7ven and Wolf try to make a deal with Gaia

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming discovers more little-known facts on a journey between Smithfields and Spitalfields, two of Londons most famous markets. He begins with a fry-up breakfast before visiting the remains of a Roman amphitheatre and investigating a mysterious secret in a Liverpool Street hotel, as well as meeting former EastEnders star Larry Lamb (Archie Mitchell)

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor heads to Tokyo and checks out the Park Hyatt, the hotel featured in the film Lost in Translation, and goes behind the closed doors of the Japanese capitals love hotels. In the fashionable district of Meguro, Richard takes in the Claska, which was the citys first boutique hotel and has its own canine beauty salon

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor explores Berlin, visiting the arty Propeller Island City Lodge - a hotel where guests can fulfil their fantasies by sleeping in lion cages, coffins or rotating beds. At the Schlosshotel in affluent Grunewald, cabaret dancer Romy Haag talks to Richard about her affair with David Bowie, who stayed at the hotel while living in the German capital in the 1970s

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: The trial of Jack McCall continues as the jury retire to mull over the evidence they have heard. Elsewhere, the townsfolk lay the outlaw Wild Bill to rest, and Bullock decides to take the law into his own hands

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: The smallpox virus threatens the very existence of the inhabitants of Deadwood, as it engulfs the entire town and leaves no one well enough to go and get any medicine. Meanwhile, Swearengen is still after Almas gold claim

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Two teenagers come to town with a tale that theyre in search of their runaway father, but when they see the two rival saloons the dollar signs ring up in their eyes and they decide to split up and each take a job at separate establishments. Meanwhile, tensions rise when Bullock returns with a fresh ultimatum for Swearengen

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: The people of the town begin to latch on to what Miles and Flora are up to, and decide its time for someone to put a stop to their games. Meanwhile, a vaccine arrives which is to be administered for smallpox

programme.title: The Penguin
programme.desc: In the wake of the death of his boss Carmine Falcone, Oz Cobb attempts to legitimise himself within Gothams criminal underworld. Crime drama spin-off from The Batman film, starring Colin Farrell

programme.title: The Penguin
programme.desc: Sofia works to secure her familys strength, while Oz attempts to play both the Falcone and Maroni families to his advantage

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: On Wallach IX, young Valya Harkonnen promises Mother Superior Raquella that shell protect the Sisterhood by putting one of their own on the imperial throne. Thirty years later, on Salusa Secundus, Valya works to combat a new threat to her long-awaited plan for a marriage between Princess Ynez-Arat and a young Richese prince. Sci-fi prequel, starring Emily Watson, Olivia Williams and Mark Strong

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: After receiving word about events on Salusa Secundus, Valya brings Theodosia to the Imperial House to help manage the situation. Meanwhile, Desmond makes a confession to Emperor Corrino, Tula reluctantly enlists Lila for a vital mission, and distrust swirls around Keiran Atreides, the sword master for House Corrino

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: Following a tragedy, the young Tula worries about being accepted despite her family name, while a sceptical Valya struggles with the decision to take the Sisterhood vow. Years later, Valya receives a message that confirms her suspicions, and Tula mourns a loss

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: After nearly all the acolytes have the same nightmare, Tula attempts to understand the origins of their disturbing dreams. Meanwhile, on the eve of the great Landsraad meeting, Valya sets her plan in motion to redeem House Harkonnen, Ynez confronts her father about rumors surrounding Desmond, and Kieran conspires to advance a rebel attack that the Sisterhood must try to thwart

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: As Corrino names a new Bashar, a Sister with deep connections to the emperor arrives on Salusa Secundus, sending ripples through the royal house. After receiving a tip, Desmond goes on a warpath to root out the insurgents, while Tula tries to keep her secret project from being discovered by the other Sisters

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: As Tula contends with the stunning revelation of Desmonds true identity, the acolytes uncover a devastating long-held secret about the Sisterhood. Meanwhile, Valya executes her plan for Ynezs escape, which leads to an epic confrontation with an increasingly powerful Desmond

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: Held captive at a secret weapons factory, Amber and Tibor narrowly escape execution by Nina before agreeing to a deal to help each other. Tibor leads Ambers husband Olly to a safe house, saving her family from Pegasus

programme.title: Euphoria
programme.desc: Rue is offered a risky deal that draws her deeper into Alamos world, while Nate battles the city council, and Maddy gives Cassie a mission that could launch her new career. Lexi enlists Jules to create an original painting for LA Nights

programme.title: The Penguin
programme.desc: Hoping to control the future of Gothams drug trade, Oz and Sofia must first address the skeletons in their closet. Victor is torn between his new life and what remains of his old

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Pembleton and Bayliss investigate the case of a man who died from internal bleeding, despite being treated at a hospital earlier that day. Lewis and Kellerman attempt to subdue someone who has gone on a rampage, and Kay tries to conceal the identity of her new boyfriend

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Egans wife is killed and his son is taken into protective custody, while Bayliss and Pembleton continue their investigation in New York. Police drama, starring Kyle Secor

programme.title: Euphoria
programme.desc: Drama offering a look at the complexities of life in a social-media obsessed world, starring Zendaya, Jacob Elordi and Sydney Sweeney

programme.title: Heated Rivalry
programme.desc: Shane and Ilya keep their secret fling alive while their growing connection is tested by personal and professional pressures

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores Newcastle, discovering strange sounds by the river and finding out how traditional culture is being reinterpreted in the Byker district. He goes behind the scenes at Newcastle United with local football hero Peter Beardsley, learns about the history of adult comic Viz from former editor Simon Donald and accompanies comedienne Sarah Millican to her favourite part of the city

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming heads across the Irish Sea to Dublin, where he succumbs to the citys famous charm and meets some of its most fascinating characters. On a journey that takes in an 18th-century pleasure house and a burlesque life-painting class, Alan also stops off in Temple Bar for a pint with singer Sharon Corr, unlocks a mystery in Irelands oldest library and uncovers a stylish secret that lurks behind the steel shutters of an old lock-up

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor returns to explore more of the worlds most luxurious hotels and begins in Miamis South Beach, home to some of the most hedonistic and extravagant establishments in America. At the Fontainebleau, he meets a man with eye-opening stories about Frank Sinatras regular visits and gets a shock when he discovers how much it costs to hire a booth in the night club. Richard also gatecrashes swimwear fashion week at the Raleigh Hotel, where he speaks to the event manager about the many celebrity parties he has organised

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: In this edition the actor explores the luxury hotels of Hong Kong. Home to more skyscrapers than any other city, he first checks in to the Mandarin in the financial district, before experiencing the Four Seasons, the only hotel with two Michelin three-star resturants, as well as the timeless Peninsula in Kowloon, which even provides a Rolls-Royce airport taxi service. He ends his stay at the Ritz-Carlton, home to the worlds highest swimming pool

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: In the final episode, Axe makes a big decision. Meanwhile, tensions rise and dynamics shift, and Connerty gets closer to the truth

programme.title: The Penguin
programme.desc: Hoping to control the future of Gothams drug trade, Oz and Sofia must first address the skeletons in their closet. Victor is torn between his new life and what remains of his old

programme.title: The Penguin
programme.desc: Sofia makes plans for a more hopeful future as she confronts the events that turned her into the Hangman and led to a decade-long fight to survive in Arkham State Hospital

programme.title: Euphoria
programme.desc: Drama offering a look at the complexities of life in a social-media obsessed world, starring Zendaya, Jacob Elordi and Sydney Sweeney

programme.title: Heated Rivalry
programme.desc: The captain of the New York Admirals has a life-changing encounter that challenges his public image and the private life hes fought to protect. Drama starring Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie about two ice-hockey players whose on-ice animosity conceals a secret romance between the two

programme.title: The Penguin
programme.desc: Hoping to control the future of Gothams drug trade, Oz and Sofia must first address the skeletons in their closet. Victor is torn between his new life and what remains of his old

programme.title: The White Lotus
programme.desc: A group of demanding vacationers arrive at a luxury resort in Sicily, where Tanya turns up with her new assistant, and a family deals with a conspicuous absence. Jennifer Coolidge and Aubrey Plaza star

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: A mass murderer is identified and the whole squad is put on 24-hour surveillance. Daniel Baldwin, Ned Beatty and Richard Belzer star

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: The detectives try to solve the murder of a girl killed in a similar style to Adena Watson. Frank demands to work on the case alone but Bayliss becomes obsessed with his theory that the two crimes are related. Richard Belzer stars

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming visits Glasgow, one of his favourite cities, to reveal unusual and often hidden sights and sounds overlooked by the public. Starting at the Necropolis, he travels to the worlds oldest music hall before enjoying the views from a city-centre landmark. He also finds the time to take tea with a film star

programme.title: The Lost Boys (1987)
programme.desc: Two teenage brothers move with their mother to a coastal town in California, where the eldest falls in with a local biker gang. He soon discovers that his new friends are vampires and he is turning into one himself, forcing his younger brother to team up with a pair of heavily armed comic shop employees to battle the undead and save his soul. Comedy horror, starring Kiefer Sutherland, Jason Patric, Dianne Wiest, Corey Feldman and Corey Haim

programme.title: Mad Max Beyond Thunderdome (1985)
programme.desc: The nomadic warrior pitches up in the city of a cut-throat desert community in search of his stolen vehicle and ends up making a deal with a local dictator to get rid of her rival for power. However, when the bargain turns sour, he is cast out into the wilderness - where he becomes the unlikely protector of a tribe of feral youngsters. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Mel Gibson, Tina Turner and Bruce Spence

programme.title: Weird Science (1985)
programme.desc: Two sexually frustrated teenagers use their computer to design the perfect woman, but are ill prepared for the outcome when their creation comes to life. She decides to make men of them, by putting them in a series of situations that will force them to stand up for themselves. John Hughes sci-fi comedy, starring Kelly LeBrock, Anthony Michael Hall, Ilan Mitchell-Smith, Robert Downey Jr and Bill Paxton

programme.title: Friendships Death (1987)
programme.desc: An android is sent to Earth by aliens on a diplomatic mission, and arrives in the middle of the the Jordanian Civil War of 1970. She forms a connection with a British reporter covering the conflict, and through him, hopes to learn to understand humanity. Sci-fi drama, starring Tilda Swinton and Bill Paterson

programme.title: The Breakfast Club (1985)
programme.desc: Five teenagers who occupy different areas of their schools social structure are thrown together for a Saturday morning detention. During the day, they analyse their behaviour and find unexpected common ground. Drama, directed by John Hughes and starring Emilio Estevez, Judd Nelson, Molly Ringwald, Anthony Michael Hall, Ally Sheedy and Paul Gleason

programme.title: E.T.: The Extra-Terrestrial (1982)
programme.desc: A lonely boy befriends an alien who has been left stranded on Earth. The two develop a psychic connection as the boy helps the extraterrestrial to contact his own people and call for rescue. However, living on Earth starts to have a devastating effect on the visitors health, and a government operative is determined to capture him for study. Steven Spielbergs sci-fi fantasy, starring Henry Thomas, Drew Barrymore, Dee Wallace and Peter Coyote

programme.title: Back to the Future (1985)
programme.desc: Teenager Marty McFly travels back to the 1950s in a time machine built by his eccentric inventor friend. The return journey proves tricky enough, but he also accidentally alters the sequence of events that led to his parents falling in love - and has to persuade his future dad to win his mothers heart, or he will never be born. Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson and Crispin Glover

programme.title: Back to the Future Part II (1989)
programme.desc: Time-travelling teenager Marty McFly travels to the 21st century, where he has to save his children from a terrible fate. However, this proves to have unforeseen consequences when the future version of arch enemy Biff sees a chance to rewrite history for his own benefit, leaving Marty stuck in a twisted parallel universe. Sci-fi comedy sequel, starring Michael J Fox and Christopher Lloyd

programme.title: Blade Runner (1982)
programme.desc: A former cop in a bleak future Los Angeles is forced to return to duty for a mission to hunt down and kill a group of renegade android replicants. The fugitives have escaped from a space colony to find their creator on Earth, in a bid to prolong their limited lifespan. Theatrical cut of Ridley Scotts sci-fi thriller, originally released in cinemas, starring Harrison Ford, Rutger Hauer, Sean Young and Daryl Hannah

programme.title: Batman (1989)
programme.desc: The masked avenger stalks the streets of Gotham City, waging a nightly war against the criminal element. However, he may have met his match when the citys crime syndicate is taken over by the crazed Joker, who plans to poison the populace with toxic chemicals. Tim Burtons comic-book adventure, starring Michael Keaton, Jack Nicholson and Kim Basinger

programme.title: E.T.: The Extra-Terrestrial (1982)
programme.desc: A lonely boy befriends an alien who has been left stranded on Earth. programme.title: Back to the Future Part II (1989)

However, this proves to have unforeseen consequences when the future version of arch enemy Biff sees a chance to rewrite history for his own benefit, leaving Marty stuck in a twisted parallel universe. Sci-fi comedy sequel, starring Michael J Fox and Christopher Lloyd

programme.title: The Naked Gun 2 1/2: The Smell of Fear (1991)
programme.desc: Blundering cop Frank Drebin discovers a secret conspiracy to kidnap a renowned solar energy expert and replace him with an imposter. As he sets out to bring the perpetrators to justice, he is unexpectedly reunited with an old flame. Slapstick comedy sequel, starring Leslie Nielsen, Priscilla Presley, George Kennedy, Richard Griffiths and OJ Simpson.

programme.title: Fanny and Alexander (1982)
programme.desc: Ingmar Bergmans Oscar-winning semi-autobiographical drama follows the lives of two children from a theatrical family in turn of the century Sweden. Their carefree lives are thrown into upheaval by the death of their father and their mothers subsequent remarriage to a puritanical minister. But refuge from their joyless new home comes in the shape of their grandmother and her lover. Starring Pernilla Allwin, Bertil Guve, Gunn Wallgren and Lena Olin

programme.title: The Natural
programme.desc: A baseball prodigys dreams of sporting stardom are cut short when he gets shot. Once he has recovered from his injuries, he rejoins his team as a rookie recruit at an age when most players are thinking of retiring - only to enjoy an unexpected run of success. Drama, starring Robert Redford, Glenn Close, Kim Basinger, Robert Duvall and Barbara Hershey

programme.title: So I Married an Axe Murderer (1993)
programme.desc: A poet has a history of ruining relationships owing to his paranoia, but when he meets the woman of his dreams, he resolves to make this romance last. However, as their wedding day approaches, he becomes convinced she is a notorious serial killer the police are hunting for. Comedy, starring Mike Myers and Nancy Travis

programme.title: Back to the Future Part III (1990)
programme.desc: Time-travelling teenager Marty McFly is transported back to the days of the Wild West, where he strives to save his eccentric scientist friend Doc Brown from death at the hands of a villainous gunslinger. Final instalment in the sci-fi comedy trilogy, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Mary Steenburgen and Thomas F Wilson

programme.title: The Naked Gun 2 1/2: The Smell of Fear (1991)
programme.desc: Blundering cop Frank Drebin discovers a secret conspiracy to kidnap a renowned solar energy expert and replace him with an imposter. As he sets out to bring the perpetrators to justice, he is unexpectedly reunited with an old flame. Slapstick comedy sequel, starring Leslie Nielsen, Priscilla Presley, George Kennedy, Richard Griffiths and OJ Simpson.

programme.title: Naked Gun 33 1/3: The Final Insult (1994)
programme.desc: Inept police detective Frank Drebin plans to retire from the force, but is lured back by the opportunity to foil a terrorist plot. Going undercover in prison, he sets out to prevent an evil mastermind from escaping and carrying out his fiendish plan - to bomb the Oscar awards ceremony. Comedy sequel, starring Leslie Nielsen, Priscilla Presley, OJ Simpson, George Kennedy and Fred Ward

programme.title: Gremlins 2: The New Batch (1990)
programme.desc: Cute furball Gizmo is reunited with his human friends Billy and Kate, who now work in the New York offices of an eccentric tycoon. However, it is not long before Gizmo has spawned a new generation of murderous carnivores, who quickly overrun the building - and a genetics lab where they produce even more startling mutations. Joe Dantes comedy sequel, starring Zach Galligan, Phoebe Cates, John Glover and Christopher Lee

programme.title: Mars Attacks! (1996)
programme.desc: The inhabitants of Earth leap into action to repel an impending invasion by a seemingly invincible Martian army, which possesses superior weaponry, a thirst for destruction and a wicked sense of humour. Tim Burtons sci-fi comedy, starring Jack Nicholson, Glenn Close, Pierce Brosnan, Annette Bening, Natalie Portman, Danny DeVito and Tom Jones

programme.title: Back to the Future Part III (1990)

Final instalment in the sci-fi comedy trilogy, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Mary Steenburgen and Thomas F Wilson

programme.title: American Pie (1999)
programme.desc: Four teenage lads make a solemn pact to lose their virginity before the high-school prom. Though their antics cause them huge embarrassment and public humiliation, the friends also learn about love and affection in their relations with the opposite sex. Coming-of-age comedy, starring Jason Biggs, Shannon Elizabeth, Chris Klein, Alyson Hannigan, Mena Suvari, Seann William Scott and Eugene Levy

programme.title: Billy Madison (1995)
programme.desc: A lazy twentysomething lives off his wealthy hotel magnate father. When the old man decides to retire, he begins to question whether his layabout son is suitable to take over the family empire and issues a challenge - he must retake 12 years of school in 24 weeks, or lose everything. Comedy, starring Adam Sandler, Bridgette Wilson, Darren McGavin and Bradley Whitford.

programme.title: Big Daddy (1999)
programme.desc: An immature 32-year-old is heartbroken when his girlfriend walks out on him. In a plan to win her back, he volunteers to look after his flatmates five-year-old son, thus demonstrating he is finally ready to grow up. However, his ideas of childcare prove unconventional and chaos quickly ensues. Comedy, with Adam Sandler, Joey Lauren Adams, Jon Stewart, Josh Mostel, Leslie Mann, Rob Schneider and Kristy Swanson, alongside Steve Buscemi in a cameo role

programme.title: Naked Gun 33 1/3: The Final Insult (1994)
programme.desc: Inept police detective Frank Drebin plans to retire from the force, but is lured back by the opportunity to foil a terrorist plot. Comedy sequel, starring Leslie Nielsen, Priscilla Presley, OJ Simpson, George Kennedy and Fred Ward

programme.title: The Brady Bunch Movie (1995)
programme.desc: It is now the 1990s and the members of the sweet, all-American family face a testing time as their brash neighbours scheme to tear down the Brady home to make way for a shopping centre. Comedy, based on the 1970s TV sitcom, starring Shelley Long, Gary Cole, Christine Taylor and Christopher Daniel Barnes

programme.title: Roofman (2025)
programme.desc: Former US Army Reserve officer Jeffrey Manchester executes a plan to rob multiple branches of McDonalds. He is eventually captured by police and becomes the first person to escape from Brown Creek Correctional Institution in North Carolina. Jeffrey hides out at a toy shop for several months, and during forays outside the building, he sparks romance with store worker Leigh Wainscott. Comedy drama, starring Channing Tatum, Kirsten Dunst and Peter Dinklage

programme.title: Dont Lets Go to the Dogs Tonight (2025)
programme.desc: An eight-year-old girl experiences life on her familys Rhodesian farm during the final stages of the Bush War. Drama, written, directed by and starring Embeth Davidtz, with Lexi Ventner and Zikhona Bali

programme.title: The Shadows Edge (2025)
programme.desc: A brilliant cyber-criminal gang evades the polices state-of-the-art surveillance system with a haul worth billions. Retired tracking expert Wong Tak-Chong is ushered back into active service to mentor rookie officer He Qiuguo and build an elite unit to stop the criminals. Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai

programme.desc: California private detective Honey ODonahue delves into a series of unusual deaths connected to a mysterious church. Co-writer/director Ethan Coens comedy mystery, starring Margaret Qualley, Aubrey Plaza and Chris Evans

programme.desc: Catherine Earnshaw is raised by her alcoholic father at a Yorkshire farmhouse, with Nelly Dean as a constant companion. Mr Earnshaw acquires an orphaned boy named Heathcliff during one of his gallivants and gifts the waif to Cathy as her pet. The children become inseparable, and as adults they embark on a destructive relationship. Writer-director Emerald Fennells drama based on Emily Brontes romance, starring Margot Robbie, Jacob Elordi and Martin Clunes

This series investigates whether the Earth is being mapped by intergalactic forces. Washington State holds the United States highest rates of UFO sightings per capita. With a new report made every 55 hours, why are UFOs choosing to hover here?

programme.title: Ancient Superstructures
programme.desc: The Castle of Chambord, the largest and most iconic French castle. Designed on the medieval model of fortified castles with its enclosure and large corner towers, this jewel is inspired by the Gothic style. The ornamentation on the upper sections reaches skywards, with the chimneys. Its silhouette is distinctive, making it one of the architectural masterpieces of the Renaissance. In this royal palace of excess, every dimension is breathtaking

programme.title: The Lost Women Spies
programme.desc: The story of Britains first female agents sent to the front line during the Second World War and spymistress Vera Atkins, who led them. In the face of danger, Vera ramped up her recruitment of female agents. Chaos ensued as the Prosper circuit in Paris neared collapse. Meanwhile, Noor, deep in Parisian resistance, struggled to maintain cover. Vera enlisted agents like Violette Szabo and Yvonne Baseden, but uncertainty shrouds their upcoming missions

programme.title: The Lost Women Spies
programme.desc: While Europe rejoiced over victory in the Second World War, Odette Hallowes struggle at Ravensbruck revealed telling information about other agents in prison. Spymistress Vera Atkins investigation intensified in Karlsruhe prison and Ravensbruck, focusing on espionage agent Noor Inayat Khans fate

programme.title: U-Boat Wargamers
programme.desc: After the fall of France in 1940, Donitzs most famous U-boat commanders embarked on a race to send British merchant ships to the bottom of the sea. Driven by a fierce competition to sink the highest tonnage, they terrorised the North Atlantic convoys and converged on them in wolfpack attacks. All the while, Vera Laughton Mathews Wrens worked around the clock to bring the battle directly to Donitzs U-boats. When confronted with unprecedented losses, and the real risk of starvation, the British Royal Navy seized on a manoeuvre developed by the famous U-boat hunter, Commander Johnny F Walker. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: Headless Romans and Anglo Saxon Gold
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits more archaeological digs around the country, finding out about a gatehouse riddled with Civil War bullets, a unique Iron Age shield made from bark, and Roman burials with pots where the heads should be

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: Mystery Shipwreck and a Roman Army Camp
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits more archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history during the prehistoric, Roman, Saxon and Tudor eras. In this edition, she examines a Dutch ship sunk by the English, a Cornish Roman fort and a 5,000-year-old Neolithic monument

programme.title: Britains Secret Islands
programme.desc: A visit to the Isle of Lewis in the Outer Hebrides, home of the 5,000-year-old standing stones of Callanish, traditional Scottish blackhouses and cut peat

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: A hidden ancient city is found in Micronesia. In New Guinea, an airplane wreck is investigated. A strange glowing forest is discovered in India, while an immense tunnel system in the Brazilian jungle is explored

programme.title: Battle 360
programme.desc: CGI reconstruction of the Second World War Pacific battle of Midway, and how American aircraft carriers Enterprise, Yorktown and Hornet turned the tide in favour of the Allies

programme.title: Bone Detectives: Britains Buried Secrets
programme.desc: Tori Herridge and a team of scientists attempt to piece together the lives behind discovered bones, and find out what happened to these people and how they died. This episode comes from Leeds, where remains of children and teenagers have been uncovered beneath a shopping arcade. Analysis of the broken bones reveals that they date back to the industrial revolution, and a thorough examination of the remains provides a detailed picture of the lives these young people would have lived during a tumultuous period of British history

programme.title: Alien Corridors
programme.desc: Patterns in UFO and UAP sightings across the world. A strange formation is seen in the sky over the sleepy suburbs of Tinley Park, Illinois. The lights return for a second time almost to the date the following year

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War
programme.desc: The Allies continued to push from the west to Berlin, while survivors recount the horrors behind finding Germanys concentration camps

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War
programme.desc: The war in the Pacific nears its violent end as a drastic tactic triggers the nuclear age, and the brave men share their thoughts behind the tragic events

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts investigate mysterious beings with big heads and eyes, whose images were found carved into rocks. Known as the greys, they are rumoured to be alien life forms

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos studies Indias Sanskrit texts, which are filled with descriptions of brain surgery, flying crafts, nuclear physics, and other advanced sciences. He learns that Hindu scholars attribute this knowledge to a time when gods walked among humans

programme.title: Hitlers Coup 1923
programme.desc: The infamous Beer Hall Putsch of November 1923, an attempt by Adolf Hitler to take power in Germany by force. Though it failed, it was a day that would change the world for ever, birthing Hitlers regime

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise and Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: A fresh look at the Third Reich from a global and current perspective, telling how the Nazis conquered Germany and then half of Europe within a few years of losing the First World War. This first episode recounts how the clash of right-wing and left-wing political convictions turned the Weimar Republic into a powder keg. The fascist movement then seized power in Italy in 1922

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: Craig and co-host Sarah Cruddas examine the Chicago OHare International Airport case of November 2006 - the sighting of a flying saucer hovering over a United Airlines airport gate. The incident was witnessed by multiple aviation professionals, pilots and passengers. The immediate aftermath of the event was captured in real time by audio recordings of communication between the airports Air Traffic Control tower and airline staff, but the sighting was dismissed by the US governments Federal Aviation Administration as a weather phenomenon. Craig and Sarah talk to UFO investigator Robert Powell, who reveals the UFO punched a circular hole in the clouds as it departed upwards after hovering over the terminal. Craig and Sarah also learn astonishing details about how aviation authorities handled the incident

programme.title: Conspiracy Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: A look at the CIAs rumoured investment in psychological operations, asking if there could be an army of remote viewers watching the American public today

programme.title: Conspiracy Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: On 8 April 2011, the FBI declassified decades of top secret government documents for the first time. Here, experts examine the possibility of the end of the world

programme.title: Blown Away

programme.title: Blown Away
