programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The Apollo 11 mission to the moon and the suggestion that the moon is actually a giant spacecraft allowing extraterrestrials to monitor Earth

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos studies Indias Sanskrit texts, which are filled with descriptions of brain surgery, flying crafts, nuclear physics, and other advanced sciences. He learns that Hindu scholars attribute this knowledge to a time when gods walked among humans

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise and Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: The invasion of Poland by the German army in 1939, examining how Hitler succeeded in pushing through his rearmament and war plans relatively unhindered

programme.title: U-Boat Wargamers
programme.desc: The remarkable story of Gilbert Roberts, Jean Laidlaw and the Womens Royal Navy Service (WRNS) - aka the Wrens. With the Allies at crisis point in 1943, pressure mounted on the Wrens to prove their worth and the stage was set for a battle that would turn the tide of the Atlantic war. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: The Lost Women Spies
programme.desc: How the capture of a key Nazi uncovered shocking realities. Amid demands for accountability, spymistress Vera Atkins and her team had to navigate murky waters to confront uncomfortable truths

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The Apollo 11 mission to the moon and the suggestion that the moon is actually a giant spacecraft allowing extraterrestrials to monitor Earth

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos studies Indias Sanskrit texts, which are filled with descriptions of brain surgery, flying crafts, nuclear physics, and other advanced sciences. He learns that Hindu scholars attribute this knowledge to a time when gods walked among humans

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts heads to a farmers field in Rutland to examine evidence of a Roman barn conversion, featuring a three-room spa, including underfloor and in-wall central heating. She also finds out about the excavation of one of Henry VIIIs lost forts in Hull, and hears from a team that discovered stunning 10,000-year-old hunting tools near Scarborough

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: Ice Age Camp and a Saint with Syphilis
programme.desc: In Scotland and northern England, Alice Roberts investigates an Ice Age camp, a mysterious medieval skeleton and the earliest evidence of salt making in Britain

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts joins archaeologists on digs of northern Britain, revealing a Roman Emperors lost bath house, the sunken treasures of medieval pilgrims and a formidable fortress perched on top of a Scottish mountain

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: Anglo Saxon Gold and Rebellious Nuns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts joins archaeologists to explore Central Englands past. She explores a dig beneath the runways of RAF Alconbury, which has uncovered a rural Roman site filled with mysteriously placed objects. In Winchester she visits a new housing development where an Anglo-Saxon cemetery has been discovered and a gold pendant has been found in a grave. She also tours a medieval nunnery near Windsor where archaeologists are attempting to learn about the women who lived within the convent

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: A Norman Panic Room and a Mesolithic Fish Trap
programme.desc: Archaeological treasures in the west of Britain, including a rare medieval cemetery, a disappearing Mesolithic landscape, a mysterious iron age burial, and the ruins of a gothic masterpiece. Presented by Alice Roberts

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Congressional UFOs, New Nessie and Zombie Fish
programme.desc: An examination of a video recently presented to Congress by the UN Navy that might prove once and for all whether UFOs really exist. Plus, alleged Loch Ness Monster footage that offers the most compelling proof of the creatures existence in years. Marine scientists also discover a mysterious Yellow Brick Road at the bottom of the Pacific Ocean

programme.title: U-Boat Wargamers
programme.desc: The remarkable story of Gilbert Roberts, Jean Laidlaw and the Womens Royal Navy Service (WRNS) - aka the Wrens. With the Allies at crisis point in 1943, pressure mounted on the Wrens to prove their worth and the stage was set for a battle that would turn the tide of the Atlantic war. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Life After People
programme.desc: Scientists explore how Earth would transform if humans vanished, here considering how the global retail machine would collapse. Shipping lanes would turn explosive and ghostly grocery stores become battlefields for rodents and predators

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Alien Golden Orb, Yeti Caught on Tape, Las Vegas Close Encounter
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things. This episode focuses on the claim that a Las Vegas family had a close encounter with 10ft-tall aliens in their backyard. Plus, the likelyhood ocean scientists discovered an unknown life form and whether a blurry video of a mysterious creature in the snow represents the best Yeti footage yet captured

programme.title: Historys Most Shocking
programme.sub-title: Leopard on the Loose, Plummeting to Earth, Elephant Attack and Balloon Chaos
programme.desc: Footage of real-life events, including a leopard on a rampage in India and a paraglider plummeting to earth having to employ his skills and fast-thinking to survive. Plus, the woman who got a little too close for comfort to an elephant and suffered the consequences

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The Apollo 11 mission to the moon and the suggestion that the moon is actually a giant spacecraft allowing extraterrestrials to monitor Earth

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Theorists suggest that some extraterrestrials may closely resemble animals, as evidenced by gods depicted with animal traits. Researchers have have contemplated that some animals may have been planted on Earth by aliens as part of an experiment to shape life on Earth

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: The Ultimate Evidence
programme.desc: Documentary exploring theories regarding supposed extraterrestrial visits to ancient civilizations, focusing on the mysterious carved blocks of the Inca ruin of Puma Punku

programme.title: The Great British Dig: History in Your Back Garden
programme.sub-title: A Prisoner of War Camp in Oldham
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis and the team visit a housing estate in Oldham, Greater Manchester, where they attempt to unearth a Victorian mill that was converted into a prisoner of war camp that housed over 6000 men during the Second World War. As part of the investigation, Hugh also visits Manor Mill, a stunning example of a 19th-century cotton mill

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts heads to a farmers field in Rutland to examine evidence of a Roman barn conversion, featuring a three-room spa, including underfloor and in-wall central heating. She also finds out about the excavation of one of Henry VIIIs lost forts in Hull, and hears from a team that discovered stunning 10,000-year-old hunting tools near Scarborough

programme.title: Mysteries from Above
programme.desc: A deep dive into some watery enigmas, such as a sunken Italian city, a unique underwater cemetery in the Florida Keys and a yellow brick road to nowhere in the depths of the South Pacific. Plus, some puzzling rocky constructions off the coast of Australia

programme.title: Mysteries Unearthed with Danny Trejo
programme.desc: The actor explores some of the deadliest discoveries of all time that were located beneath the earth, frozen in the tundra, or hidden in the depths of the sea

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: A fire-breathing crater burns in the middle of a desert - and some suspect it is a gateway to Hell. Scientists try to work out how an eight-foot-long Bull Shark ended up in a golf course water feature. They want to know if a sharknado event lifted the creature out of the sea and dumped it inland. A photographer captures an ominous ring hovering in the sky over downtown Chicago

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: A father and his children are being filmed as a bolt of lightning mysteriously strikes at them from a clear blue sky. Scientists try to work out why a football stadium dome, thought to be indestructible, catastrophically collapses. A giant ice creature is spotted in Alaska, while a jet has a close encounter with a UFO

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: In the Gulf of Mexico, a shark is attacked and eaten by a mysterious predator. Biologists look at the footage to work out if a prehistoric arthropod has risen from the deep. Plus, a drone over a dense Russian forest films a sinister cloud rising up out of a mysterious machine

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Lancaster Castle has witnessed scenes of historical, cultural and political significance throughout the centuries, including incidents of religious persecution, the trials of the Lancashire Witches and 200 public executions. Owned by the Duchy of Lancaster, it was a state prison until 2011 and is still a working crown court. Dan Jones explores its turbulent history and meets Wendy Robinson, a retired prosecutor from Australia, whose ancestors were sentenced to death at Lancaster Crown Court in 1817 for stealing sheep

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Arundel Castle, in West Sussex, has been the seat of the Dukes of Norfolk and their ancestors for over 850 years, with its fortunes rising and falling with those of the Howard family. A particularly low point occured when Thomas Howard, the fourth Duke of Norfolk, was executed by Elizabeth I for allegedly plotting to marry her rival Mary Queen of Scots. Dan Jones meets a weapons expert, who shows him how Richard FitzAlan, the third Earl of Arundel, would have fought in the 100 Years War. He also meets a social historian, who gives an insight into how Arundel would have prepared for Queen Victorias visit in 1846

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Photographs of a 25-mile structure known as the eye of the Sahara, and an extraordinary cloud emanating from an island in the Indian Ocean

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: The series examines six more mysterious satellite images, including a possible smokescreen over North Korea, and two forgotten pyramids in Egypt

programme.title: Storage Wars

programme.title: Storage Wars

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the Norman period of British history by examining events that occurred in Winchester, a historic town that still retains many hallmarks of the 11th and 12th centuries. To get a taste of this era, she attempts to storm a castle wearing chain mail, investigates the origins of modern surgery, and tries some eel pie. At the conclusion of the episode, cutting edge CGI is used to take the town back in time, and recreate how it would have looked during this period of British history

programme.desc: The broadcaster invites viewers to join him as he takes an after-hours stroll around one of his favourite places, the Natural History Museum. But this is no ordinary tour as some of the many exhibits spring to life as the veteran naturalist strolls along

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Following his visit to the Great Barrier Reef in 1957, naturalist and broadcaster David Attenborough returns and uses the latest filming techniques to unlock the secrets of the natural wonder. In the first edition, he descends beneath the waves at night and uses cutting-edge technology to generate computer scans of the sea floor. He also learns about a complex community of animals, as well as the annual coral spawning, which is responsible for the great diversity in the reef

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Using the latest technology, the naturalist explores the shark-infested waters of Osprey Reef in the Triton submersible, before returning to the nesting grounds of the Green Sea Turtle, 60 years after his first visit to Raine Island. He also marvels at manta ray cleaning stations on Lady Elliot Island, and tracks dwarf minke whales to discover their surprising reasons for returning, and why the reef is vital for their survival

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The naturalist examines what the next few decades may hold for this remarkable ecosystem, and looks at the work of scientists who are trying to preserve the area and the species that inhabit it, assisted by research vessel the Alucia. He also embarks on an ambitious attempt to dive further into the reef than ever before to witness the resilient coral that live down in the depths and see if they hold answers that may help to overcome challenges that lie ahead, as the impact of humans on the reef continues to take its toll

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa begins to feel increasingly neglected by Homer and tries to worm her way into his affections by feigning an interest in American football. programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Mrs Krabappel tires of her lonely life and searches for love in the personal ads, unaware that Bart knows of her plight and sees it as the perfect opportunity to make mischief. Meanwhile, Homer promises to stop swearing if Ned Flanders shaves off his moustache

programme.title: Chicken Run (2000)
programme.desc: A headstrong chicken and her fellow fowls ponder how to escape from a grim Yorkshire farm before the evil farmers wife can turn them into pies. programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.sub-title: Three Men and a Comic Book

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The naturalist examines what the next few decades may hold for this remarkable ecosystem, and looks at the work of scientists who are trying to preserve the area and the species that inhabit it, assisted by research vessel the Alucia. He also embarks on an ambitious attempt to dive further into the reef than ever before to witness the resilient coral that live down in the depths and see if they hold answers that may help to overcome challenges that lie ahead, as the impact of humans on the reef continues to take its toll

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Global Adventure
programme.desc: Documentary capturing some of the greatest filming sequences of David Attenboroughs career, using previously unseen footage, from the deepest dives in a submersible on the Great Barrier Reef to watching a million bats take off in a cave in Borneo. Other highlights include life in the frozen tundra of South Georgia, a colony of King Penguins and the worlds largest migration of Hammerhead sharks in the Galapagos Islands

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Global Adventure: Home Planet
programme.desc: Further footage of David Attenboroughs global wildlife adventures, from the depths of the Great Barrier Reef to the ancient rainforests of Borneo

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Global Adventure: The Rise of Nature
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the rise of life on Earth, from the emergence of the first creatures on seabeds hundreds of millions of years ago to the wildlife of today

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Dragons and Damsels
programme.desc: Arguably more beautiful than butterflies, better fliers than hummingbirds and with intriguing and complex behavioural patterns - dragonflies and damselflies are marvels of the insect kingdom. This documentary uses the latest camera technology to show their manoeuvres, mating strategies, territorial behaviour and flight dynamics, revealing them to be the ultimate insect

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A further selection of creepy-crawly close-ups, focusing on courtship in the bug world. Highlights include scorpions shaking their booty, the perfume-wearing nasonia wasp and the tropical cricket, for whom romance goes out of the window as it uses bribery to seal the deal with would-be partners. One potential partner to steer well clear of, however, is the praying mantis, the female of which is notorious for eating the male just after - or even during - mating. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A close-up view of sex, bug-style, as David Attenborough talks viewers through the different ways in which creepy-crawlies reproduce. Size matters for the minuscule male orb spider, creepily sneaking up on its intended and trying to mate without her noticing, while theres no rest for the lothario-like butterfly, which has plenty of notches on its proverbial bedpost. However, the harvestman spider has no use for sex at all, and reproduces by cloning itself

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough shifts the focus to bugs that prefer co-operation rather than conflict. They include burrowing cockroaches, the suitably named social spiders - which share a 30-metre web - the queen bee and her happy hive of workers and green ants pulling together to build a nest

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Kingdom of Plants
programme.desc: The naturalist and film-maker explores the many varieties of plant life housed at the Royal Botanic Gardens in Kew, west London, and explains what they reveal about the natural world and the development of life on Earth. He begins inside the establishments Palm House, whose climate has been engineered to simulate conditions found in rainforests, charting the evolution of plants that grow in the worlds wettest regions and investigating how flowering plants spread across the globe so rapidly

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Kingdom of Plants
programme.desc: The naturalist explores how the plants at Kew Gardens change through the seasons and demonstrates the surprising properties of several species, including the predatory pond-dwelling bladderwort utricularia and a fungus that appears to be capable of mind control. He also discusses the largest single organism on the planet, visits the worlds biggest flower and explains how plants and insects can communicate using scents and music

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Kingdom of Plants
programme.desc: The naturalist concludes his exploration of the Royal Botanic Gardens in Kew, west London, by examining plants that have evolved to minimise their dependence on water, enabling them to survive in the worlds driest environments. He witnesses a cactus blooming at midnight, tells the story of the spectacular flowering of the Agave franzosinii, and visits the Millennium Seed Bank, a facility designed to save plants from future extinction

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the Galapagos, a collection of volcanic islands in the Pacific known for its extraordinary wildlife. More than 150 years after Charles Darwin formulated his ground-breaking theory of evolution on the remote archipelago, Attenborough explains how life on the islands has continued to evolve in biological isolation, examining how the ever-changing volcanic landscape has given birth to species and sub-species that exist nowhere else in the world. He begins by revealing how Galapagos rose from the ocean four million years ago

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster reveals how life has learnt to adapt quickly in the ever-changing volcanic landscape, resulting in species that exist nowhere else in the world. David investigates the driving forces behind their evolution, exploring the appearance and behaviour of remarkable creatures and vegetation including giant whale sharks, marine iguanas and seemingly normal dandelion seeds that grow into plants the size of trees

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his exploration of the archipelago with a look at how, despite their proximity, no two of the 19 islands are the same. He also uncovers evidence that evolution on Galapagos is occurring faster than Charles Darwin could ever have imagined, and encounters Lonesome George, the last remaining giant Pinta Island tortoise, who died just a few weeks after filming in June 2012

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist examines the world of flight, travelling across the globe to chart the evolutionary story of those creatures who seemingly defy the rules of gravity by taking to the air. Initially focusing on insects, Attenborough meets a descendant of the dragonfly, before examining the Atlas beetle, which tends to restrict flight to times when it needs a mate or a fresh supply of food. More aesthetically pleasing is a group of beautiful tropical butterflies, followed by an in-depth study into the blow fly - the jet fighter of insects

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist visits the jungles of Borneo to meet creatures that like to glide through life - the harlequin tree frog and the little-known colugo - often referred to, incorrectly, as a flying lemur. He also has a CGI-assisted encounter with the first true vertebrate flyer, the pterosaur, receives a flying lesson from a small flock of swans at Loch Lomond in Scotland and encounters the planets largest species of bat

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist turns his attention to creatures that dominate the skies today. He begins with the vast bird family, watching a group of vultures as they go scavenging and some peregrine falcons dive bombing for their prey. He then moves on to the floating forests of Ecuador to find out what makes the humble hummingbird tick, before ending his journey at the Gomantong bat caves of Borneo - a place he first filmed in 1972 - witnessing a mass exodus of the creatures

programme.title: Secret World of Sound with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the ways different creatures hear and produce sound and how it can influence their decisions, using cutting-edge audio technology to reveal a new perspective and understanding of animal behaviour

programme.title: Secret World of Sound with David Attenborough
programme.desc: From melodic songs to strange hums, David Attenborough shares the sounds that gibbons, lyrebirds, bull elk and more use to find a mate - and warn off the competition

programme.title: Secret World of Sound with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A look at how baby animals communicate in their quest for survival, using sound to beg and call for help in the early days of life

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Elephant
programme.desc: Naturalist David Attenborough investigates the life of Jumbo, a famous Victorian elephant, supposedly the largest that ever lived. Jumbo arrived at London Zoo in 1865 and was popular with the royal family, but was dangerously unhappy in captivity. In later life, he would be sold to PT Barnums circus, becoming a celebrity before dying under mysterious circumstances. Attenborough examines Jumbos remains and considers the lives of elephants in the wild to learn more about the animals difficult life

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Natural History Museum Alive
programme.desc: The broadcaster invites viewers to join him as he takes an after-hours stroll around one of his favourite places, the Natural History Museum. But this is no ordinary tour as some of the many exhibits spring to life as the veteran naturalist strolls along

programme.title: The Bachelor King
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the lives of king penguins, filmed on the sub-Antarctic island of South Georgia over five months. The programme imagines what one penguins life would be, following it during the challenging, awkward time between adolescence and adulthood. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Following his visit to the Great Barrier Reef in 1957, naturalist and broadcaster David Attenborough returns and uses the latest filming techniques to unlock the secrets of the natural wonder. In the first edition, he descends beneath the waves at night and uses cutting-edge technology to generate computer scans of the sea floor. He also learns about a complex community of animals, as well as the annual coral spawning, which is responsible for the great diversity in the reef

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Using the latest technology, the naturalist explores the shark-infested waters of Osprey Reef in the Triton submersible, before returning to the nesting grounds of the Green Sea Turtle, 60 years after his first visit to Raine Island. He also marvels at manta ray cleaning stations on Lady Elliot Island, and tracks dwarf minke whales to discover their surprising reasons for returning, and why the reef is vital for their survival

programme.title: Reef Wrecks
programme.desc: An exploration of shipwrecks and the ecosystems they support, beginning with those located off the coast of Bonaire, an island in the Caribbean

programme.title: David Attenboroughs First Life
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part documentary, the naturalist explores the origins of life on Earth. Beginning the journey near his childhood home in Charnwood Forest, Leicestershire, he learns how a fossil discovery transformed scientists understanding of the evolution of complex organisms, and in Newfoundland and Australia he reveals the earliest kinds of animal to exist on the planet. With the help of cutting-edge technology and visual effects, the programme brings the creatures to life, demonstrating how they survived

programme.title: David Attenboroughs First Life
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the two-part documentary, the naturalist travels to Burgess Shale in Canadas Rocky Mountains, where he explores how evolution gained pace once creatures developed mouths and mobility. In Scotland, he reveals that one of the smallest fossils found holds the secret to how animals began surviving on dry land, and explains the bloom of life during the oxygen-rich Carboniferous period

programme.title: Attenborough at 100: Behind the Lens
programme.desc: As David Attenborough turns 100, this intimate film presents new interviews, behind-the-scenes footage and extraordinary clips

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his close-up examination of the creepy-crawly world, shifting focus to the bugs ultimate invention - the colony. Key residents of these minuscule mega-cities include honey bees, termites, leafcutter ants - who have a knack for farming - and their Argentine cousins, members of a community so vast it spans an entire continent

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The making of David Attenboroughs documentary series charting battles and rivalries within the world of bugs

programme.title: Africas Wild Horizons
programme.desc: Hidden within the Kalahari Desert and pulsating with natural beauty, the Okavango attracts scores of animals to its lush waters

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Elderly marmoset Jenny gets a visit from the vet before the arrival of a new life partner. Orangutan youngster Hujan begins to adjust in the nursery

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Alison travels to Hampshire to rescue Clyde, a pet-trade marmoset. Young orangutan Hujan, meanwhile, meets older male Bulu in the nursery

programme.title: Amazing Animal Friends
programme.desc: A look at creatures that form unlikely bonds at rescue centres, including a North American raccoon that has found a friend in a South American coati

programme.title: Guardians of the Wild
programme.desc: A look at how the lifeless Liuwa Plains National Park, having been ravaged by poachers, is now being restored to its former glory by a remarkable team of conservationists

programme.title: Volcanic Odysseys
programme.desc: With approximately 130 active volcanos, volcanologist Tom Pfeiffer leads an expedition to Indonesia - arguably the most active place on Earth

programme.title: Secrets of the Enchanted Forests
programme.desc: Exploring the planets most diverse woodlands and learning how the forests connect and nurture the lives of countless species

programme.title: Surviving the Amazon
programme.desc: A jaguar stalks the river, a snake makes a killer move and a stealth tarantula waits to strike. Nothing is safe when the Amazons predators are on the prowl

programme.title: Dawn to Dusk
programme.desc: Wildlife in the tropical Pacific, where Komodo dragons fight for leftovers and fiddler crabs joust on the beach. Under the waves, manta rays and walking sharks patrol the deep

programme.title: After the Wildfires
programme.desc: In 2020, Australia went up in flames. The scale and intensity signified an undeniable new phase in the global climate crisis. For Australias wildlife, who have adapted to severities of weather, it was cataclysmic. And for those who did survive, the real challenge only now begins. This documentary examines the toll, the recovery and the healing of the wild after Australias wildfires

programme.title: Fast and Furious 7 (2015)
programme.desc: After defeating Owen Shaw and his crew and securing amnesty for their past crimes, Dom, Brian and the rest of the team return home to live normal lives again. However Shaws older brother Deckard - a rogue special forces assassin vows revenge on Dom, who he holds responsible for leaving Owen in a coma. Action thriller sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Jason Statham and Paul Walker in his last film role

programme.title: Fast and Furious (2009)
programme.desc: News of a friends murder brings international fugitive Dominic Toretto back to his home city of Los Angeles in search of revenge. It is not long before his pursuit leads him to cross paths with FBI agent Brian OConner, and despite their hostile feelings toward each other, the pair work together to bring down those responsible. Thriller sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Paul Walker, Michelle Rodriguez and Jordana Brewster

programme.title: Fast and Furious 5 (2011)
programme.desc: Criminal Dominic Toretto is broken out of prison by his partners in crime and together they flee to South America. They begin assembling a gang of crooks to carry out a heist in Rio, but the FBIs top manhunter is in the country and hot on their trail. Action adventure sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Dwayne Johnson, Paul Walker and Jordana Brewster

programme.title: Fast and Furious 6 (2013)
programme.desc: A gang of fugitives is reunited by an FBI manhunter, who offers the criminals pardons in return for their help. They must go to Europe and bring down a criminal syndicate led by a ruthless London gangster, whose right-hand woman is an old friend of theirs assumed to be dead. Action thriller sequel, with Vin Diesel, Dwayne Johnson and Michelle Rodriguez

programme.title: Fast and Furious 7 (2015)
programme.desc: After defeating Owen Shaw and his crew and securing amnesty for their past crimes, Dom, Brian and the rest of the team return home to live normal lives again. However Shaws older brother Deckard - a rogue special forces assassin vows revenge on Dom, who he holds responsible for leaving Owen in a coma. Action thriller sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Jason Statham and Paul Walker in his last film role

programme.title: Fast and Furious 8 (2017)
programme.desc: Street racing criminal Dominic Toretto is drawn out of retirement by an encounter with a ruthless cyberterrorist, who recruits him for an international criminal scheme. As he sets out to steal the technology she needs to unleash chaos on the world, he is drawn into conflict with both old enemies and his former gang. Action adventure sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Charlize Theron, Dwayne Johnson and Kurt Russell

programme.title: The Fast and the Furious (2001)
programme.desc: In a move to identify suspected hijackers, an undercover police officer infiltrates the high-octane world of illegal late-night street racing by rival gangs. He is befriended by a champion driver, but when he also forms a bond with the racers sister, he is pitched into a conflict of loyalties. Thriller, starring Vin Diesel, Paul Walker, Michelle Rodriguez and Jordana Brewster

programme.title: 2 Fast 2 Furious (2003)
programme.desc: A disgraced former cop is arrested by the FBI for illegal street racing, but his one-time colleagues offer him freedom in exchange for going on an undercover mission. Teaming up with an old racing friend and a customs official, he has to infiltrate and bring down a drug-trafficking operation. Thriller sequel, with Paul Walker reprising his role from The Fast and the Furious alongside Tyrese Gibson, Eva Mendes and Cole Hauser

programme.title: The Fast and the Furious: Tokyo Drift (2006)
programme.desc: An American teenager is sent to live with his father in Tokyo after a brush with the law, but soon becomes involved in illegal street racing. When he ends up owing money to gangsters after losing a race against a driver with Yakuza connections, he has to learn to master the Japanese style of driving to win and pay back his debts. Action adventure, starring Lucas Black, Nathalie Kelley and Bow Wow

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Tealc, Carter and Daniel return to Stargate Command, where they report a mysterious fifth member of SG-1 is trapped on the planet with Jack - a story that Hammond views with suspicion. Sci-fi drama, starring Richard Dean Anderson

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: The teams wormhole triggers an ecological disaster that threatens a friendly planet, and ONeill discovers its usual guardians, the Asgard, are unable to help, as offering assistance would violate a treaty with the Goauld and jeopardise other protectorates. Sci-fi drama, starring Richard Dean Anderson

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Part one of two. Daniel Jackson of SG-1 uncovers a pointer to the whereabouts of the Lost City of the Ancients, and Dr Weir prepares her team to venture into the distant Pegasus Galaxy. When they eventually reach their goal, however, they find themselves facing a struggle for survival in a hostile and unfamiliar land. First episode of the spin-off from Stargate SG-1, starring Torri Higginson

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Part two of two. Unable to return to Earth, Weir and the team go in search of an alternative fuel source. They come across a small settlement but receive a cold reception from the inhabitants, who live in constant fear of an approaching enemy

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: One of the Athosian children accidentally unleashes what appears to be a harmless entity during a game. However, everyone soon begins to realise the mysterious being is anything but innocuous

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The teams ship is damaged while fleeing from the Wraith and gets stuck halfway through the Stargate, leaving the crew less than an hour to find a way of dislodging it. To make matters worse, an alien parasite attaches itself to Sheppards neck and begins to suck the life from him

programme.title: Escape from New York (1981)
programme.desc: Former special forces soldier Snake Plissken is sentenced to life on Manhattan Island, an anarchic penal colony for the most dangerous criminals. When Air Force One is hijacked and crashes there, Snake is coerced into rescuing the US president from prisoners holding him hostage. John Carpenters sci-fi action adventure, with Kurt Russell, Donald Pleasence, Lee Van Cleef, Adrienne Barbeau and Isaac Hayes

programme.desc: Cassandra suddenly develops uncontrollable telekinetic powers, which threaten both her own future and that of the team. Sci-fi drama, starring Richard Dean Anderson, Michael Shanks and Collen Rennison

programme.title: Star Trek: Voyager
programme.desc: Part one of two. Annorax, the Krenims desperate leader, vows to manipulate time to alter the course of history and wipe out the enemy that annihilated his own colony - and quite possibly take out Captain Janeways starship in the process. Sci-fi adventure, starring Kate Mulgrew and Robert Beltran

programme.title: Star Trek: Enterprise
programme.desc: Tucker is stunned to discover he is pregnant after sharing an intimate moment with a female engineer aboard a stricken alien ship. Sci-fi drama, starring Connor Trinneer

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Cassandra suddenly develops uncontrollable telekinetic powers, which threaten both her own future and that of the team. Sci-fi drama, starring Richard Dean Anderson, Michael Shanks and Collen Rennison

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Nick and Hank battle a powerful Wesen who has become a vigilante carrying out deadly punishments for human criminals. Meanwhile, Juliette learns the truth about Nicks mother, and Adalind has an ultrasound with unusual results

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: The Governor prepares his forces for war as the second meeting with Ricks prison group draws near, and with all hope of a peaceful resolution seemingly gone, Andrea decides she must warn her old friends they are about to walk into a trap. However, when she escapes from Woodbury, the towns brutal leader finds a new point of focus for his fury. Post-apocalyptic drama, starring David Morrissey and Laurie Holden

programme.title: Intergalactic
programme.desc: When law-abiding space cop Ash Harper is convicted of a crime she did not commit, she is sentenced to exile and loaded onto prison spaceship the Hemlock. Her mother Rebecca, who is head of galactic security, is determined to prove her daughters innocence and bring her safely home, but any hopes of a reunion are dashed when Ashs fellow convicts overthrow the guards and hijack the Hemlock. Sci-fi drama, starring Savannah Steyn and Natasha OKeeffe

programme.desc: In 1933, the Harvard Professor Arthur Kingsley Porter went missing after spending the night on the small Donegal Gaeltacht island of Inishbofin. programme.title: You Rang, MLord?

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.sub-title: The Chief, the Cook, His Mum and Her Lodger

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon makes plans to take Tracey and Dorien on a madcap 1970s-themed weekender, but Tracey is about to receive some eye-opening news that could spoil her fun. First of a two-part episode of the comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: As Tracey awaits the results of her scan, she and Sharon swap home truths in the comfort of a spa, and try to make plans for the future, while Dorien prepares to say a long goodbye to her daughter Naomi. Second of a two-part episode of the comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph, with Frances Ruffelle and Michael Lumsden

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: As the comedy returns, theres bad news for the Stubbs family as they hear that Traceys ex-husband has died and she has to decide what kind of send-off to give him. Linda Robson, Pauline Quirke and Lesley Joseph star, with appearances from Spandau Ballets Martin Kemp and one-time EastEnder Jamie Foreman (Derek Branning)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The family are devastated when unscrupulous burglars take advantage of their absence at Darryls funeral to ransack the house. But rather than involve the Old Bill, they decide to track down their possessions themselves, setting out on a trail that takes them from a snooker parlour and a pawnbrokers to an infamous local drag club. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon disapproves of Tracey dating policeman Barry, especially as she appears to be neglecting Garths feelings about his dear, departed dad, leading to a major argument between the sisters. Can Dorien solve their problems in her new role as a magazine agony aunt? Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: A couple of the birds are forced to fly this week when one of them becomes fed up of being treated like a doormat. The pair of them are soon holed up together in Sharons old bedsit, where they decide to take a united stand - but things are never quite that straightforward. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph, with a guest appearance from Kate Williams (Love Thy Neighbour)

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The renowned Dr Katherine Talbot (Claire Goose) is slated to speak at a gathering of the League of Women Voters. Unfortunately, her presence isnt welcome by all and her impassioned speech is interrupted when a bomb explodes injuring fellow womens suffrage advocate Clara Martin and killing a mysterious man. Meanwhile, the professional tension between Murdoch and coroner Violet Hart is exacerbated by what he deems a slow investigation. Crime drama, starring Yannick Bisson

Neil Morrissey guest stars alongside Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby

programme.desc: The leader of an organisation dedicated to eradicating human trafficking is killed before making an important speech, leading the agents to suspect the killer was desperate to stop the presentation. Self-proclaimed psychic Kristina Frye joins the team to help with the investigation, and soon discovers the victim was going to expose a shocking truth about his own association. Guest starring Leslie Hope (24) and Eugene Byrd (Heroes), with Simon Baker

programme.title: Hudson and Rex
programme.desc: Charlie and Rex are on the hunt after a prison van crash frees convicted killer Danny Winters. After tracking the fugitive, Charlie and Rex find themselves held hostage

programme.title: Unforgettable
programme.desc: Al is determined to find the killer of his former mentor and partner Jim Kelly, but when stolen files are seized by police, he must come to terms with the fact that his hero may not have been the man he seemed. Carries investigation leads the team to a drug dealer, suggesting that Kelly had some dubious associates, and giving Al an uphill struggle to save the deceaseds reputation

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A hen party on the island ends in tragedy when the bride-to-be is found dead in her bath the following morning. It doesnt take long for Humphrey to work out she was murdered - and when the electronic key proves she was the last person to open the door, it looks like she knew her killer. But why was the body in the bath? Meanwhile, Camille has a big decision to make. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring Amy Nuttall, William Ash and Ali Bastian

programme.desc: Mallory is forced to turn to the sleuthing priest for help when a senior member of the police force is murdered at a bowls match. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The renowned Dr Katherine Talbot (Claire Goose) is slated to speak at a gathering of the League of Women Voters. Unfortunately, her presence isnt welcome by all and her impassioned speech is interrupted when a bomb explodes injuring fellow womens suffrage advocate Clara Martin and killing a mysterious man. Meanwhile, the professional tension between Murdoch and coroner Violet Hart is exacerbated by what he deems a slow investigation. Crime drama, starring Yannick Bisson

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: When a dockworker is assassinated, Murdoch pursues elusive witness Robert Parker and learns anti-union thugs may be involved. His concern is the murderer is poised to kill again if not arrested first. Crime drama, starring Yannick Bisson

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A death at an investment event leads Crabtree to a savvy visionary who happens to be the father he never knew, but Murdoch suspects he may be culpable. Drama, with Yannick Bisson

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: An adventure in the woods turns scary when Murdoch, Ogden, Higgins and Ruth are stalked by a beast that killed two men. Crime drama, starring Yannick Bisson

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The case of a murdered man with a collection of unusual stamps gets complicated for Murdoch when another detective is implicated in the crime. Crime drama, starring Yannick Bisson

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Determined to bolster his income, Higgins moonlights as a cab driver, shuttling drunks, scoundrels, louts and - it appears - a dead passenger. Crime drama, starring Lachlan Murdoch

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A schoolgirls killing brings Gently into the alien world of pop and media celebrity when it turns out the victims best friend is a rising TV star. Bacchus suspects the dead girls music teacher, since rumours persist that she was having an affair with him. But when it seems everyone has a different opinion of the girl, Gently must uncover these different faces to get to the truth of her murder. Neil Morrissey guest stars alongside Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When the father of a young writer is found dead, tensions mount against the travellers in Kembleford, and the priest races to find out who actually committed the murder before one of them is wrongly sent to the gallows. Starring Mark Williams, Nathalie Buscombe and Philip McGinley

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest and his friends find themselves stranded with a group of hedonists and are desperate to escape, fearful that their lives are in danger. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as a Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A body is discovered in a vineyard and Father Brown is tasked with searching for the murderer among a group of suspects who are all seeking to reinvent themselves. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest becomes embroiled in an audacious art heist masterminded by his adversary Flambeau, who intends to use Father Brown and his friends to help him settle an old score. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: When an American PI is arrested for murder, Frank and Lu are drawn into the world of motors in search of a valuable muscle car and are taken for a ride by some shifty characters

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: Lu infiltrates a local slimming club to discover their secrets, but gets more than she bargained for when she realises her sister is a member. Mark Benton and Jo Joyner star

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Goodfellow turns to Father Brown when he witnesses Mallory frame an innocent man. Mark Williams and John Burton star in the mystery drama, based on the stories by GK Chesterton

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: When a skeleton is found bricked up inside the wall of an old house, Murdoch must track down a killer who thought they got away with it

programme.desc: Crime series that revolves around the special partnership between new detective Mark Hudson, who has recently joined SJPDs Major Crimes Division, and Rex, an extraordinary former K9 dog. Mark and Rex must hunt down a key witness against a notorious crime boss before a hired assassin finds him first

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Veteran rock band the Flowers of Progress are on the island to record a new album after years of being apart, and with a major sponsorship deal hanging in the balance, tensions are running high. However, their reunion proves to be short-lived when lead singer Stevie Smith is electrocuted in the swimming pool. It seems the victim had a stormy relationship with his brother and fellow band member Jim, while there are rifts between the others and more than one has an axe to grind. But how could any of them have done the deed when they were all in the studio at the time? To add to Humphreys problems, he is still finding his feet without Camille, although the arrival of new officer JP helps lighten Dwaynes workload. Neil Morrissey and Sally Phillips guest star

programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc and Lou Sanders are joined by Rylan Clark, Lucy Beaumont and Fatiha El-Ghorri as Rylan has a sticky run-in with a pigeon and Lucy reveals her knicker-nicking past

programme.desc: US host Sean Kelly oversees UK bidders in Essex battling to win potential treasures found in storage containers, lock-ups and garages

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson goes off-road as he test-drives the Range Rover, while the Lotus Esprit is put through its paces on the test track. Plus, a students beloved Metro receives a new look and the winner of the reasonably priced car competition is announced. With Richard Hammond and Jason Dawe

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The Smart Roadster and Vauxhalls VX220 Turbo come under the expert gaze of Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May. Plus, an attempt to fit a 1985 Jaguar XJS with a nitrous oxide injection kit. Actor and former footballer Vinnie Jones is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: A journey around the region, examining the challenges currently facing the nations in the area. Simon begins in Malta, uncovering the countrys reputation for organised crime, before moving on to the southern coast of Italy, where he joins police officers exploring underground tunnels used by crime bosses on the run. Finally, he arrives in Albania and discovers an ancient tradition of revenge where children can pay the price for their parents actions

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon explores the eastern Mediterranean, beginning in Cyprus, where he goes on patrol with UN peacekeepers overseeing an abandoned buffer zone. He moves on to Lebanon, where he discovers the roots of Christianity, and Israel, where he boards a navy vessel guarding the countrys offshore oil reserves. Finally, he crosses the worlds most heavily fortified borders into Gaza, where he meets a woman helping reconstruction efforts by making bricks into ash

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul takes Bob on a steam train to the north of England to search for a prized English salmon, fishing in the rivers Eden and Tyne in search of their elusive quarry. They are joined on the riverbank by former footballer Paul Gascoigne, who has also always dreamt of catching a salmon, while Dr Anand Patel has a chat with Bob about how to look out for the more vulnerable at Christmastime

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer head to Mid-Wales to fish for chub in the Irfon and Wye rivers. The friends also meet David Lyons, the founder of charity Tackling Minds, which helps people with depression, anxiety or who are recovering from addiction to get out to rivers and lakes to fish

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers: Mums Know Best
programme.desc: Simon King and David Myers present a celebration of Britains most enduring home-cooking techniques, touring the nation in search of family recipes that have been passed down through generations, but are now in danger of being lost. The pair begin by visiting three Scottish mothers, and learning how to make a variety of treats including cheese pie, samosas and rumpy pumpy soup

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers: Mums Know Best
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate the traditional British picnic, meeting mothers who consider themselves experts at creating home-cooked delicacies for enjoying in the great outdoors. The pair reveal their own take on the Scotch egg, and demonstrate a complex Victoria sponge recipe. Plus, a Birmingham mum prepares a selection of spicy Indian treats

programme.desc: Officers discover a fuel tanker with bundles of cocaine hidden in the cab, and three men with fake IDs and a gruesome video of a murder on their phones are questioned

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: Britains most talented detectives uncover human trafficking in Bristol, hunt the leader of a county lines gang in Surrey, and trawl through hours of CCTV to stop a vaccine fraudster in London

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Katherine Parkinson hosts an extended episode of the satirical quiz, with comedian Julian Clary and journalist Jemima Kelly joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of rest and recreation this week, with Stephen K Amos, Susan Calman, Lou Sanders and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts an edition of the comedy quiz show. Regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians James Acaster, Tom Allen, Ari Eldjarn, Ed Gamble and Ellie Taylor. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Topical comedy quiz show, hosted by Dara O Briain. Stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie is left feeling ill after a flu outbreak, but the government official insists on making a presentation about her proposal for the towns harvest festival. Meanwhile, Ron and Andy have a day of male bonding, and Tom goes to a spa. Comedy, starring Amy Poehler

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie decides to bury a time capsule about life in Pawnee, but receives an odd suggestion from a local man, which causes unexpected consequences. comedy, starring Amy Poehler

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: A journey around the region, examining the challenges currently facing the nations in the area. Simon begins in Malta, uncovering the countrys reputation for organised crime, before moving on to the southern coast of Italy, where he joins police officers exploring underground tunnels used by crime bosses on the run. Finally, he arrives in Albania and discovers an ancient tradition of revenge where children can pay the price for their parents actions

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon explores the eastern Mediterranean, beginning in Cyprus, where he goes on patrol with UN peacekeepers overseeing an abandoned buffer zone. He moves on to Lebanon, where he discovers the roots of Christianity, and Israel, where he boards a navy vessel guarding the countrys offshore oil reserves. Finally, he crosses the worlds most heavily fortified borders into Gaza, where he meets a woman helping reconstruction efforts by making bricks into ash

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul takes Bob on a steam train to the north of England to search for a prized English salmon, fishing in the rivers Eden and Tyne in search of their elusive quarry. programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy from Messrs Barker and Corbett, including the Chemist and music by the New Sensation

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy from Messrs Barker and Corbett, including the Limerick Writers Convention and the Gravediggers Wedding

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy from Messrs Barker and Corbett, including News Interrupted by Jackanory and music by the Nolans

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy from Messrs Barker and Corbett, including Strange Ice-Cream Flavours and Two Men Discussing Creation at the Pub

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Ronnie Corbett and Ronnie Barker star in a festive edition of their celebrated comedy show, first aired in 1984. West End star Elaine Paige joins in the sketches, jokes and songs, featuring the Ballad of Snivelling and Grudge and A Film Story with Peter Wyngarde as Sir Guy. Patrick Troughton and John Owens also appear

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Zoologist and broadcaster Miranda Krestovnikoff walks along the Deben in Suffolk, from Sutton Hoo, famous for its archaeological treasures, to Ramsholt Quay on the coast. Along the way she passes salt marshes, tidal lagoons and wooded groves, exploring the rivers history and wildlife and meeting people whose lives are shaped by it

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales return to Wales to explore a canal that has eluded them for more than half a century. The Monmouthshire and Brecon canal is cut off from the rest of Britains network of waterways by the Black Mountains, and meanders its way through the tranquil Usk valley. Along the way the couple visit the old ironworks at Blaenavon, where children as young as five were once put to work, and lend a hand with some lock restoration work. They also explore the castle at Tretower - which is rumoured to be haunted

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales travel down the River Nile from Luxor to Aswan. Tim has some misgivings about attempting such a daring journey, but is won over by Prus desire for fresh adventure. They discover how travel along the river was vital to the ancient Egyptian civilisation, and discover how the first ever canals were constructed nearby to ferry building materials for the temple at Karnak. Along the way, they visit a Nubian village, purchase aphrodisiacs in a souk, and dance by moonlight

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales explore the Naviglio Grande Canal in Italy, which runs from Lake Maggiore to Milan and is Europes oldest working canal, providing passage for marble-carrying barges for over 500 years. Along the way, they stop at the Swiss lakeside town of Locarno, the gardens of Isola Bella, and Milans most famous cathedral, as well as learning a traditional Italian barging song

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales explore the waterways of North-West England, from the suburbs of Manchester, through Britains first purpose-built canal, to the port at Ellesmere. They discover how canals played a vital role in transforming Britain during the Industrial Revolution, and have subsequently become a central point of Manchesters gay community

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales return to Wales to explore a canal that has eluded them for more than half a century. The Monmouthshire and Brecon canal is cut off from the rest of Britains network of waterways by the Black Mountains, and meanders its way through the tranquil Usk valley. programme.title: Great Canal Journeys

Tim has some misgivings about attempting such a daring journey, but is won over by Prus desire for fresh adventure. They discover how travel along the river was vital to the ancient Egyptian civilisation, and discover how the first ever canals were constructed nearby to ferry building materials for the temple at Karnak. programme.title: Thailand: Earths Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Exploring the wildlife and culture of the Southeast Asian nation. The first episode examines the south of the country, a popular tourist destination for Westerners, and examines the role of spirituality in daily life. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Thailand: Earths Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: In central Thailands forests, fertile plains and even its city streets, nature finds a way of living alongside people, and spirituality can be found in human and animal relationships, both likely and unlikely. This bustling region is known as the nations rice bowl - but even here, there are magical places to be found. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Thailand: Earths Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: The mountains and forests of northern Thailand, an area that is home to a variety of wildlife, leading to unusual partnerships developing between animals and people. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Patagonia: Earths Secret Paradise
programme.desc: A glimpse at the rarely seen South American wilderness, home to a range of creatures from terrain as varied as the Andes to Cape Horn. Cameras reveal the secret lives of pumas and hummingbirds, soar with condors over glacial peaks and explore monkey puzzle forests dating back to the time of the dinosaurs. The extreme kayakers who gain thrills and excitement from traversing the areas raging waterfalls are observed, as are the native cowboys, known as gauchos, as they round up wild horses. Narrated by Santiago Cabrera

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: The king continues to publicly dispute the legitimacy of the high court but after two days of witness testimonies, the evidence against him is overwhelming. When Charles enters the courtroom on the third day, he is prepared to compromise but it is too late - he is found guilty of treason and is sentenced to be executed

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright is reluctant to buy a new suit until Nurse Gladys Emmanuel persuades him to. He agrees, but only if I can be made in the same style as his old one. Roy Clarkes classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker, David Jason and Lynda Baron

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.sub-title: An Errand Boy by the Ear
programme.desc: Arkwright decides Granville needs toughening up and starts dragging him around the store by his ear, but the regular customers are unhappy by what they see

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Comedy sketches with the popular funnyman, featuring Kevin and Perry, Wayne and Waynetta Slob and the Self-Righteous Brothers. With special guests Desmond Lynam, Jimmy Hill and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Christmas is just around the corner and Mikes mum Betty is staying - and testing Mike and Alisons patience with her interfering ways. Theres no easy way to ask her to go home so the couple - and the ghosts - struggle on, with Button House feeling fuller than ever. Now a convert, Robin is missing that all-important Christmassy feeling, so the gang members share their secrets to getting into the festive spirit - but when even more decorations and merry music dont do the trick, the ghosts wonder if Robin will be able to capture that Christmas magic again

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Alma bags herself a talent agent and lands an acting role that turns out to be completely demoralising. Meanwhile, Lin is back in hospital and has discovered a penchant for witchcraft, while Almas best mate Leanne has transformed a truck into a bar thats become Boltons biggest hot-spot. Comedy, written by and starring Sophie Willan, with Jayde Adams, Lorraine Ashbourne and Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: In a bid to get her granddaughter off her case, Joan advises Alma to have some fun as her low self-esteem is affecting her posture. Alma gets a job at Leannes bar and meets a potential love interest, but neither goes to plan. Comedy, written by and starring Sophie Willan, with Jayde Adams, Lorraine Ashbourne and Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Alma decides its time to start focusing on herself and her career so she joins a stand-up comedy course run by her old acting teacher Ian, who is now a comedian and global star after one of his videos went viral. Meanwhile, Joan receives news that her mother Alice has died and her estranged siblings gather to plan the funeral. Guest starring Steve Pemberton and Julie Hesmondhalgh

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary goes to the pub to drown his sorrows, so Dermot invites Deborah to their flat for a romantic evening of candlelight, dinner and music. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Rodney aspires to be a pop star, taking on the role of the drumless drummer in Mental Mickeys band but, not surprisingly, Del-Boy manages to ruin his chances. With a guest appearance by DJ Mike Read, alongside David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: The Trotters agree to dog-sit while Boycie and Marlene go on holiday, and happily tuck into the fresh meat lovingly provided for their charge, scarcely suspecting what lies ahead. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del pays a stripper to attend a party at the Nags Head with Rodney, who has been bet by Mickey Pearce that he cannot find a date for the occasion. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Always one to jump on the latest passing bandwagon, Del-Boy goes eco-friendly and decides to create a better world for baby Damien and his generation - by starting his own bottled water business. programme.title: Open All Hours: Inside Out
programme.desc: Diane Morgan narrates this special documentary about the much-loved comedy, featuring contributions from Sir David Jason, Johnny Vegas, Tim Healy, Ronnie Barkers daughter Charlotte, and more

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granvilles idea to open a new catering arm, offering his customers tasty toasties, doesnt quite go according to plan when his reconditioned toaster blows a fuse. Mr Newbold gets a little bolder in his pursuit of Mrs Rossi, while trying to avoid the Black Widow. Leroy and Willis meet Beth and Ruby with a view to going on a date, but Willis is worried that Rubys not right for him. David Jason, James Baxter, Johnny Vegas and Geoffrey Whitehead star in veteran writer Roy Clarkes much-loved comedy

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright is reluctant to buy a new suit until Nurse Gladys Emmanuel persuades him to. He agrees, but only if I can be made in the same style as his old one. Roy Clarkes classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker, David Jason and Lynda Baron

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Mr Cholmondley-Warner offers his idiosyncratic views on madness, while Stan and Pam flash their cash in Spain. Kevin refuses to go to bed, Tim gets engaged to a royal and Alf Git meets an old flame at a funeral with explosive results

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: The Wobbly Randy Old Ladies take a trip to the petrol station to fill the car up. Frank Doberman lectures Damon Hill on abiding by the Highway Code, and the Scousers visit London and get a lot more than they bargained for. Comedy sketches, featuring Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Alma has been dreaming of meeting her long-lost father ever since she was a little girl, it played out in her imagination like a black and white movie. After the death of her great-granny and Joans ongoing situation, Alma feels it might be time to make that dream a reality. She tracks down her father and spends time with him? but can reality really live up to the movies in her mind?

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Joan has more shocking news and as she struggles to come to terms with her life and unachieved dreams, she writes a bucket list and demands Alma and Lin help her to complete it. The three generations of Nuthall women take a nostalgic trip to Blackpool, while Alma takes Joan to watch her perform live comedy

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot is sacked for dangling a customer by the ankles, while Gary, on the other hand, is offered a new post. However, he has difficulty breaking the news to George and Anthea. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes

programme.desc: Comedy, starring Nigel Havers and Tony Britton as the father-and-son doctors, one an idealist NHS GP, the other a money-mad Harley Street consultant

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The disappearance of an illicit tin of pineapple chunks leaves the inmates horrified that there is a thief in their midst - and for a while, no one is above suspicion. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Marlene discovers the farmhands concocting a virility potion for Rocky the bull, and manages to sneak a dose into Boycies coffee. programme.title: Dads Army
programme.desc: Frazer is asked to look after the platoons Lewis gun, but finds himself in hot water when he misplaces the spring - and fears it is has been left inside a coffin. Second World War comedy first shown in 1969, starring John Laurie, Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to enter an art competition, but no one is prepared for the results of his creative endeavours. The shopkeeper also finds himself with a box full of mysterious tins from China, and he and Leroy get to grips with the challenge of selling something when they do not know what it is

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright has a rival for Nurse Gladys Emmanuels attention, as a visitor from Australia - and an old flame of hers- returns to his old stomping ground

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee Macks not having much luck with lights these days, whether its the fizzling sign at the start of this show, the blackout during his recent Sheffield gig, or the dimly lit area at the start of this seventh series. Lee and Lucy have enjoyed a spot of culture at their local cinema, but the sparkle of a French movie is soon diminished when the romantic walk home finds them in an imposing underpass. She has her handbag stolen by a bunch of cocky youths from under his nose. Given the fact the layabout failed to protect her, or come up with an accurate description of their muggers, Lee feels the urge to prove his manliness, but will a spell at the local boxing ring be a help or a hindrance? Sally Bretton and Katy Wix also star

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy announces that she plans to marry Pavlov, a mechanic from the old Eastern bloc who needs her help to stay in the country and avoid returning to his tragic life back home. However, Lee is the only one who seems to object to the arrangement. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton, Tim Vine and Timothy West

programme.title: Almas Not Normal

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Christmas is just around the corner and Mikes mum Betty is staying - and testing Mike and Alisons patience with her interfering ways. Theres no easy way to ask her to go home so the couple - and the ghosts - struggle on, with Button House feeling fuller than ever. Now a convert, Robin is missing that all-important Christmassy feeling, so the gang members share their secrets to getting into the festive spirit - but when even more decorations and merry music dont do the trick, the ghosts wonder if Robin will be able to capture that Christmas magic again

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Alma bags herself a talent agent and lands an acting role that turns out to be completely demoralising. Meanwhile, Lin is back in hospital and has discovered a penchant for witchcraft, while Almas best mate Leanne has transformed a truck into a bar thats become Boltons biggest hot-spot. Comedy, written by and starring Sophie Willan, with Jayde Adams, Lorraine Ashbourne and Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Alma decides its time to start focusing on herself and her career so she joins a stand-up comedy course run by her old acting teacher Ian, who is now a comedian and global star after one of his videos went viral. programme.title: Still Open All Hours

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright has a rival for Nurse Gladys Emmanuels attention, as a visitor from Australia - and an old flame of hers- returns to his old stomping ground

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee Macks not having much luck with lights these days, whether its the fizzling sign at the start of this show, the blackout during his recent Sheffield gig, or the dimly lit area at the start of this seventh series. Lee and Lucy have enjoyed a spot of culture at their local cinema, but the sparkle of a French movie is soon diminished when the romantic walk home finds them in an imposing underpass. She has her handbag stolen by a bunch of cocky youths from under his nose. Given the fact the layabout failed to protect her, or come up with an accurate description of their muggers, Lee feels the urge to prove his manliness, but will a spell at the local boxing ring be a help or a hindrance? Sally Bretton and Katy Wix also star

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy announces that she plans to marry Pavlov, a mechanic from the old Eastern bloc who needs her help to stay in the country and avoid returning to his tragic life back home. However, Lee is the only one who seems to object to the arrangement. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton, Tim Vine and Timothy West

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Tim Vine. Tim arrives home after a night out clubbing and realises he has picked up the wrong coat, one which contains a pocket full of drugs. After discovering the owner is Larry the Butcher Stubbs, he enlists Lees help to return the jacket without ending up in serious danger

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy commits a minor indiscretion while drunk, but claims to have no memory of the event. Determined to get to the bottom of the affair, she and Lee embark on an ambitious quest to discover the truth about whether it is possible to retain their memories of a heavy night of drinking - making use of liberal amounts of home-made potato hooch. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Alma has been dreaming of meeting her long-lost father ever since she was a little girl, it played out in her imagination like a black and white movie. After the death of her great-granny and Joans ongoing situation, Alma feels it might be time to make that dream a reality. She tracks down her father and spends time with him? but can reality really live up to the movies in her mind?

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Lin has got herself in quite the legal tangle, so Alma decides to channel Erin Brockovich to get her mum out of trouble and bags her a pro bono solicitor. After the court hearing, Alma is left questioning everything and unsure what to do next, Jim tells her to write down all her family stories and all her experiences and use them in her stand-up routines
